content.text:
Please analyze this codebase and create a CLAUDE.md file, which will be given to future instances of Claude Code to operate in this repository.

What to add:
1. Commands that will be commonly used, such as how to build, lint, and run tests. Include the necessary commands to develop in this codebase, such as how to run a single test.
2. High-level code architecture and structure so that future instances can be productive more quickly. Focus on the "big picture" architecture that requires reading multiple files to understand.

Usage notes:
- If there's already a CLAUDE.md, suggest improvements to it.
- When you make the initial CLAUDE.md, do not repeat yourself and do not include obvious instructions like "Provide helpful error messages to users", "Write unit tests for all new utilities", "Never include sensitive information (API keys, tokens) in code or commits".
- Avoid listing every component or file structure that can be easily discovered.
- Don't include generic development practices.
- If there are Cursor rules (in .cursor/rules/ or .cursorrules) or Copilot rules (in .github/copilot-instructions.md), make sure to include the important parts.
- If there is a README.md, make sure to include the important parts.
- Do not make up information such as "Common Development Tasks", "Tips for Development", "Support and Documentation" unless this is expressly included in other files that you read.
- Be sure to prefix the file with the following text:

```
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
```

file.content:
# personal-claude-docs

Personal Claude Code knowledge base. Shared across all computers via GitHub.

---

## Architecture

```
GIT REPOS                        CONTENT
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ClaudeDocs                       enterprise/ commands, skills,
(personal-claude-docs)      ──►  learning, reference, agents
                                 users/emarquez-personal/ prefs

ClaudeHome                       CLAUDE.md (home loader)
(~/.claude/ on home)        ──►  @enterprise + @personal + home paths
                                 settings.json, memory/

ClaudeWork                       CLAUDE.md (work loader)
(~/.claude/ on work)        ──►  @enterprise + @personal + work context
                                 settings.json, memory/

ONEDRIVE (same personal account - both computers)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
01_Claude_Home/                  Personal Obsidian vault
00_Claude/                       Work Obsidian vault
```

```
HOME COMPUTER  (eduar / D:\OneDrive\)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ~/.claude/  (ClaudeHome repo)                       │
│    commands/ ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/commands
│    skills/   ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/skills
│    learning/ ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/learning
│    reference/──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/reference
│    enterprise/─symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/
│    personal/ ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/users/emarquez-personal/
│                                                      │
│  01_Claude_Home/  (ACTIVE vault - wired)             │
│    _claude-commands ──symlink──► ~/.claude/commands  │
│    _claude-skills   ──symlink──► ~/.claude/skills    │
│    _claude-learning ──symlink──► ~/.claude/learning  │
│    _claude-reference──symlink──► ~/.claude/reference │
│                                                      │
│  00_Claude/  (on disk via OneDrive, NOT wired)       │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

WORK COMPUTER  (emarquez / C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ~/.claude/  (ClaudeWork repo)                       │
│    commands/ ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/commands
│    skills/   ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/skills
│    learning/ ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/learning
│    reference/──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/reference
│    enterprise/─symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/
│    personal/ ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/users/emarquez-personal/
│                                                      │
│  00_Claude/  (ACTIVE vault - wired)                  │
│    _claude-commands ──symlink──► ~/.claude/commands  │
│    _claude-skills   ──symlink──► ~/.claude/skills    │
│    _claude-learning ──symlink──► ~/.claude/learning  │
│    _claude-reference──symlink──► ~/.claude/reference │
│                                                      │
│  01_Claude_Home/  (on disk via OneDrive, NOT wired)  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Setup Script Ownership

| Script | Lives in | Responsibility |
|--------|----------|---------------|
| `setup-personal.ps1` | ClaudeDocs | Wire `~/.claude/` → ClaudeDocs content only |
| `setup-home.ps1` | ClaudeHome (`~/.claude/.setup/`) | Call setup-personal + wire `01_Claude_Home` → `~/.claude/` |
| `setup-work.ps1` | ClaudeWork (`~/.claude/.setup/`) | Call setup-personal + wire `00_Claude` → `~/.claude/` |

### CLAUDE.md Loading Order (every session)

```
~/.claude/CLAUDE.md
  @enterprise/CLAUDE.md   ←  Boris Cherny practices, workflow (this repo)
  @personal/CLAUDE.md     ←  Eduardo's preferences, tech stack (this repo)
  ## Machine section       ←  local paths only
```

On work computer, the work-specific section (Safari Circuits, ADO, BeFirst) follows the personal layers.

---

## Computers

| Computer | User | OneDrive | Active Vault | Claude repo |
|----------|------|----------|--------------|-------------|
| Home | `eduar` | `D:\OneDrive\` | `01_Claude_Home` | ClaudeHome |
| Work | `emarquez` | `C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\` | `00_Claude` | ClaudeWork |
| Laptop | `{user}` | auto-detect | `01_Claude_Home` | ClaudeHome or new |

Both vaults (`01_Claude_Home` and `00_Claude`) exist on **every computer** via OneDrive sync.
Only the active vault for that computer is wired to `~/.claude/`.

---

## New Machine Setup

```powershell
# 1. Clone repos
git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeDocs.git "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs"

# Home computer
git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeHome.git "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude"
& "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\.setup\setup-home.ps1"

# Work computer
git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeWork.git "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude"
& "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\.setup\setup-work.ps1"
```

> `setup-home.ps1` and `setup-work.ps1` each call `setup-personal.ps1` internally,
> then wire their respective vault. One script, full setup.

---

## Daily Workflow

```powershell
# After adding a learning, skill, or command:
cd ~/personal-claude-docs
git add .claude/enterprise/ .claude/users/
git commit -m "Learn: what you discovered"
git push

# On another computer:
git pull
# No re-run needed - symlinks already point here
```

---

## Folder Guide

| Folder | Purpose | How to add |
|--------|---------|-----------|
| `enterprise/commands/` | Custom `/commands` | Add `{name}.md` |
| `enterprise/skills/` | Reusable workflows | Add `{name}/SKILL.md` |
| `enterprise/learning/best-practices/` | Validated knowledge | Add `{topic}.md` |
| `enterprise/learning/ideas/backlog.md` | Quick capture | Append to file |
| `enterprise/reference/` | Quick lookup docs | Add `{topic}.md` |
| `enterprise/agents/` | Subagent definitions | Add `{name}.md` |
| `enterprise/scripts/` | Verification tools | verify-sync.ps1, etc. |
| `users/emarquez-personal/` | Personal overrides | Edit CLAUDE.md |
| `scripts/setup-personal.ps1` | Wire `~/.claude/` | Run from ClaudeHome/Work setup |
| `scripts/setup-work-template.ps1` | Template for ClaudeWork | Copy to ClaudeWork `.setup/` |

---

➜ = symlink/junction  
✅ = synced via this method  
❌ = not synced via this method

### Documents NOT Synced (Machine-Local Only)

| Path | Purpose | Home | Work | Sync? |
|------|---------|------|------|-------|
| `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` | Auto-generated loader | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ (generated per machine) |
| `~/.claude/state/` | Session state | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ (local only) |
| `~/.claude/worktrees/` | Task worktrees | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ (local only) |
| `~/.claude/.setup/` | Machine scripts | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ (copied from template) |
| `~/.claude/projects/{project}/memory/` | Project memory | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ (per-project, local) |

### File Sync Methods Summary

| Content | Primary Sync | Secondary | Claude Reads From |
|---------|--------------|-----------|-------------------|
| Commands | **Git** (personal-claude-docs) | OneDrive (vault mirror) | `~/.claude/commands/` |
| Skills | **Git** (personal-claude-docs) | OneDrive (vault mirror) | `~/.claude/skills/` |
| Learning | **Git** (personal-claude-docs) | OneDrive (vault mirror) | `~/.claude/learning/` |
| Agents | **Git** (personal-claude-docs) | None | `~/.claude/agents/` |
| Scripts | **Git** (personal-claude-docs) | None | Direct from repo |
| Enterprise CLAUDE.md | **Git** (personal-claude-docs) | None | Via @-import |
| Personal CLAUDE.md | **Git** (personal-claude-docs) | None | Via @-import |
| Session state | None (local) | None | `~/.claude/state/` |
| Memory | None (local) | None | `~/.claude/projects/*/memory/` |

**Key Point:** Always use **Git** as the primary sync method. The OneDrive vault mirrors are for editing in Obsidian, but git push/pull is authoritative.

## 🔍 Quick Command Reference

```powershell
# Verify sync is working
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\scripts\verify-sync.ps1" -Detailed

# Add a new command and sync
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git add .claude/enterprise/commands/new-command.md
git commit -m "Add /new-command"
git push

# Pull latest changes on other computer
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git pull
# Restart Claude Code session to use new command

# Check which vault is wired (work computer only)
Test-Path "C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\00_Claude\04-Commands\_claude-commands"
# Should return True on work computer

# Re-run setup if symlinks break
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\scripts\setup-personal.ps1" -Force
```

   109→    $canSymlink = $isAdmin -or $devMode

   113→Write-Host "  Link method: $linkMethod" -ForegroundColor $(if ($canSymlink) { "Green" } else { "Yellow" })

   127→# ─── 3. Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   128→
   129→function Set-Link {
   130→    param([string]$LinkPath, [string]$Target, [string]$Label)
   131→
   132→    if (-not (Test-Path $Target)) {
   133→        Write-Warn "$Label : target missing ($Target)"
   134→        return
   135→    }
   136→
   137→    if (Test-Path $LinkPath) {
   138→        $existing = Get-Item $LinkPath -Force
   139→        $isLink   = $existing.Attributes -band [IO.FileAttributes]::ReparsePoint
   140→
   141→        if ($Force) {
   142→            if ($isLink) {
   143→                # Remove junction/symlink without touching target contents
   144→                try {
   145→                    [System.IO.Directory]::Delete($LinkPath)
   146→                } catch {
   147→                    try { Remove-Item $LinkPath -Force -ErrorAction Stop }
   148→                    catch { Write-Warn "$Label : could not remove existing link - $_"; return }
   149→                }
   150→            } else {
   151→                # Real directory - backup if has content, delete if empty
   152→                $isEmpty = (@(Get-ChildItem $LinkPath -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)).Count -eq 0
   153→                if ($isEmpty) {
   154→                    Remove-Item $LinkPath -Force -Recurse
   155→                } elseif ($BackupExisting) {
   156→                    $bk = "$LinkPath.bak-$(Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd-HHmmss')"
   157→                    Rename-Item $LinkPath $bk
   158→                    Write-Ok "$Label : backed up to $(Split-Path $bk -Leaf)"
   159→                } else {
   160→                    Write-Warn "$Label : real directory with content - use -Force -BackupExisting to replace"
   161→                    return
   162→                }
   163→            }
   164→        } else {
   165→            if ($isLink) { Write-Skip "$Label : already linked" }
   166→            else         { Write-Warn "$Label : real directory exists (use -Force to replace)" }
   167→            return
   168→        }
   169→    }
   170→
   171→    $parent = Split-Path $LinkPath -Parent
   172→    if (-not (Test-Path $parent)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $parent -Force | Out-Null }
   173→
   174→    if ($existing.PSIsContainer -or (Test-Path $Target -PathType Container)) {
   175→        # Directory link
   176→        if ($canSymlink) {
   177→            New-Item -ItemType SymbolicLink -Path $LinkPath -Target $Target -Force | Out-Null
   178→        } else {
   179→            cmd /c mklink /J "$LinkPath" "$Target" 2>&1 | Out-Null
   180→        }
   181→    } else {
   182→        # File link
   183→        if ($canSymlink) {
   184→            New-Item -ItemType SymbolicLink -Path $LinkPath -Target $Target -Force | Out-Null
   185→        } else {
   186→            Copy-Item $Target $LinkPath -Force
   187→            Write-Warn "$Label : copied (file symlinks need admin - re-run as admin for live link)"
   188→            return
   189→        }
   190→    }
   191→
   192→    if (Test-Path $LinkPath) { Write-Ok "$Label" }
   193→    else                     { Write-Fail "$Label : creation failed" }
   194→}

   289→Set-Link $projectMemoryLink $projectMemorySrc "projects (synced via git)"
   290→
   291→# ─── 8. Safari Circuits Integration (work only) ──────────────────────────────
   292→
   293→if ($Profile -eq "work") {
   294→    Write-Host ""
   295→    Write-Step "Setting up Safari Circuits organizational integration"
   296→
   297→    $orgDocsPath = "$env:USERPROFILE\organizational-docs"
   298→
   299→    if (Test-Path $orgDocsPath) {
   300→        # Symlink to organizational .claude config
   301→        $safariClaudeSrc = "$orgDocsPath\.claude"
   302→        Set-Link "$claudeRoot\safari" $safariClaudeSrc "safari (org config)"
   303→
   304→        # Symlink to shared standards (branding, API, security, etc.)
   305→        $safariSharedSrc = "$orgDocsPath\shared"
   306→        $referenceDir = "$claudeRoot\reference"
   307→        if (-not (Test-Path $referenceDir)) {
   308→            New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $referenceDir -Force | Out-Null
   309→        }
   310→        Set-Link "$referenceDir\safari" $safariSharedSrc "reference/safari (org standards)"
   311→
   312→        Write-Host "  Safari standards available via @safari/CLAUDE.md" -ForegroundColor DarkGray
   313→        Write-Host "  Reference materials at ~/.claude/reference/safari/" -ForegroundColor DarkGray
   314→    } else {
   315→        Write-Warn "safari : organizational-docs not found at $orgDocsPath"
   316→        Write-Host "  Clone from Safari Circuits GitHub if needed" -ForegroundColor Gray
   317→        Write-Host "  The @safari layer will be skipped until available" -ForegroundColor Gray
   318→    }
   319→} else {
   320→    Write-Skip "Safari integration : not needed on $Profile computer"
   321→}
   322→
   323→# ─── 9. Install .setup/ scripts ──────────────────────────────────────────────
   324→
   325→Write-Host ""
   326→Write-Step "Installing .setup/ scripts"
   327→
   328→$setupDir     = "$claudeRoot\.setup"
   329→$scriptsSrc   = $PSScriptRoot
   330→
   331→if (-not (Test-Path $setupDir)) {
   332→    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $setupDir -Force | Out-Null
   333→}
   334→
   335→foreach ($scriptName in @("claude-session.ps1", "sidebar.ps1")) {
   336→    $src  = Join-Path $scriptsSrc $scriptName
   337→    $dest = Join-Path $setupDir   $scriptName
   338→    if (Test-Path $src) {
   339→        Copy-Item $src $dest -Force
   340→        Write-Ok ".setup/$scriptName"
   341→    } else {
   342→        Write-Warn ".setup/$scriptName : source not found at $src"
   343→    }
   344→}
   345→
   346→# ─── 10. PowerShell profile shortcut ─────────────────────────────────────────
   347→
   348→Write-Host ""
   349→Write-Step "Setting up PowerShell profile shortcut"
   350→
   351→try {
   352→    $profilePath = $PROFILE
   353→    if ([string]::IsNullOrEmpty($profilePath)) {
   354→        $profilePath = "$env:USERPROFILE\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1"
   355→        Write-Warn "profile : `$PROFILE is empty, using default: $profilePath"
   356→    }
   357→
   358→    $snippet = @'
   359→
   360→# ── Claude Code ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   361→function claude-session {
   362→    param([string]$Project = "")
   363→    & "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\.setup\claude-session.ps1" @PSBoundParameters
   364→}
   365→Set-Alias cs claude-session
   366→'@
   367→
   368→    $profileDir = Split-Path $profilePath -Parent
   369→    if (-not (Test-Path $profileDir)) {
   370→        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $profileDir -Force | Out-Null
   371→    }
   372→
   373→    if (Test-Path $profilePath) {
   374→        $existing = Get-Content $profilePath -Raw -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
   375→        if ($existing -match "claude-session") {
   376→            Write-Skip "profile : claude-session already present"
   377→        } else {
   378→            Add-Content -Path $profilePath -Value $snippet -Encoding utf8
   379→            Write-Ok "profile : shortcut appended to $profilePath"
   380→        }
   381→    } else {
   382→        Set-Content -Path $profilePath -Value $snippet.TrimStart() -Encoding utf8
   383→        Write-Ok "profile : created $profilePath with shortcut"
   384→    }
   385→    Write-Host "  Run 'cs' or 'claude-session' to launch with sidebar." -ForegroundColor DarkGray
   386→    Write-Host "  Reload with: . `$PROFILE" -ForegroundColor DarkGray
   387→} catch {
   388→    Write-Warn "profile : Could not set up PowerShell profile shortcut - $_"
   389→    Write-Host "  You can manually add 'claude-session' function later if needed" -ForegroundColor DarkGray
   390→}
   391→
   392→# ─── 11. Verification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   393→
   394→Write-Host ""
   395→Write-Host "  Verification" -ForegroundColor White
   396→
   397→$checks = @(
   398→    @{ Path = "$claudeRoot\commands";   Label = "commands  " },
   399→    @{ Path = "$claudeRoot\skills";     Label = "skills    " },
   400→    @{ Path = "$claudeRoot\learning";   Label = "learning  " },
   401→    @{ Path = "$claudeRoot\reference";  Label = "reference " },
   402→    @{ Path = "$claudeRoot\agents";     Label = "agents    " },
   403→    @{ Path = "$claudeRoot\enterprise"; Label = "enterprise" },
   404→    @{ Path = "$claudeRoot\personal";   Label = "personal  " },
   405→    @{ Path = "$claudeRoot\projects";   Label = "projects  " },
   406→    @{ Path = "$claudeRoot\CLAUDE.md";  Label = "CLAUDE.md " }
   407→)
   408→
   409→# Add Safari checks for work computer
   410→if ($Profile -eq "work") {
   411→    $checks += @(
   412→        @{ Path = "$claudeRoot\safari";           Label = "safari    " },
   413→        @{ Path = "$claudeRoot\reference\safari"; Label = "safari-ref" }
   414→    )
   415→}
   416→
   417→$allOk = $true
   418→foreach ($c in $checks) {
   419→    if (Test-Path $c.Path) {
   420→        $item = Get-Item $c.Path -Force
   421→        $tag  = if ($item.Attributes -band [IO.FileAttributes]::ReparsePoint) { "link" } else { "real" }
   422→        Write-Host "    OK [$tag] $($c.Label)" -ForegroundColor Green
   423→    } else {
   424→        Write-Host "    XX       $($c.Label) MISSING" -ForegroundColor Red
   425→        $allOk = $false
   426→    }
   427→}
   428→
   429→Write-Host ""
   430→if ($allOk) {
   431→    Write-Host "  All links OK. Claude Code will use personal-claude-docs on next session." -ForegroundColor Cyan
   432→} else {
   433→    Write-Host "  Some links missing. Re-run with -Force or check errors above." -ForegroundColor Yellow
   434→    exit 1
   435→}
   436→Write-Host ""
   437→

<system-reminder>

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

# Learning to CLAUDE.md Enforcement Pipeline

## Overview

This pipeline ensures that best practices learned from videos, research, and experience are systematically promoted into CLAUDE.md files where they become **enforced team standards**.

```
┌─────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐
│   LEARN         │────▶│   DOCUMENT       │────▶│   PROMOTE       │────▶│   ENFORCE        │
│                 │     │                  │     │                 │     │                  │
│ - YouTube       │     │ - best-practices/│     │ - Review        │     │ - CLAUDE.md      │
│ - Articles      │     │ - youtube-       │     │ - Validate      │     │ - Skills         │
│ - Experience    │     │   analyses/      │     │ - Approve       │     │ - Hooks          │
└─────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘     └─────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘
```

## Stage 1: LEARN

**Input:** Raw content (videos, articles, trial-and-error)

**Actions:**
1. Watch/read content
2. Extract key insights
3. Note Safari-specific relevance

**Output:** Mental model of the practice

## Stage 2: DOCUMENT

**Input:** Key insights from learning

**Actions:**
1. Create file in `youtube-analyses/` or update `best-practices/{domain}.md`
2. Use standard templates with YAML metadata
3. Tag with `implementation_status: documented`
4. Rate `safari_relevance: high|medium|low`

**Output:** Documented practice with metadata

## Stage 3: PROMOTE

**Input:** Documented practice with `safari_relevance: high`

**Promotion Criteria:**
- [ ] Practice is validated (tested in real work)
- [ ] Practice has clear enforcement rules
- [ ] Practice applies across multiple projects
- [ ] Team agrees this should be standard

**Actions:**
1. Update `implementation_status: validated`
2. Write enforcement rule in promotion format:
   ```markdown
   ## [Practice Name]

   **Do:** [What to do]
   **Don't:** [What not to do]
   **Why:** [Reasoning]
   **Source:** [Link to learning doc]
   ```
3. Add to `~/.claude/learning/promoted/` queue

**Output:** Promotion-ready practice

## Stage 4: ENFORCE

**Input:** Promotion-ready practice

**Target Locations:**

| Scope | File | When to Use |
|-------|------|-------------|
| Global (all projects) | `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` | Universal practices |
| Safari projects | `safari-trace/.claude/CLAUDE.md` | Safari-specific |
| Specific domain | Project CLAUDE.md | Project-specific |

**Enforcement Levels:**

| Level | Format in CLAUDE.md | Claude Behavior |
|-------|---------------------|-----------------|
| **MUST** | "ALWAYS do X" / "NEVER do Y" | Claude will follow strictly |
| **SHOULD** | "Prefer X over Y" | Claude will default to this |
| **MAY** | "Consider X when..." | Claude will use judgment |

**Output:** Enforced practice in CLAUDE.md

## Promotion Template

When adding to CLAUDE.md, use this format:

```markdown
### [Practice Name]

**Rule:** [MUST|SHOULD|MAY] [action]

**Do:**
- Specific action 1
- Specific action 2

**Don't:**
- Anti-pattern 1
- Anti-pattern 2

**Why:** [Brief explanation]

**Source:** [Link to learning/best-practices/{file}.md]
```

## Tracking Promotions

Update `implementation-tracking/status.md` when promoting:

```markdown
| Practice | Source | Status | Promoted To | Date |
|----------|--------|--------|-------------|------|
| Component naming | ui-design.md | Enforced | global CLAUDE.md | 2026-01-18 |
```

## Quick Commands

### Promote a Practice
1. Verify practice in `best-practices/`
2. Write enforcement rule
3. Add to appropriate CLAUDE.md
4. Update tracking status

### Review Promoted Practices
```bash
# List all enforced rules across CLAUDE.md files
grep -r "MUST\|SHOULD\|NEVER\|ALWAYS" ~/.claude/*.md
grep -r "MUST\|SHOULD\|NEVER\|ALWAYS" ~/safari-trace/.claude/*.md
```

## Example Promotion

### Before (in best-practices/ui-design.md):
```markdown
## Component Naming
Use PascalCase for component names. Prefix Safari-specific components with "Safari".
Status: validated
```

### After (in CLAUDE.md):
```markdown
### Component Naming Convention

**Rule:** MUST use PascalCase for React components

**Do:**
- `SafariButton.tsx`
- `UserProfileCard.tsx`
- `DataTable.tsx`

**Don't:**
- `safari-button.tsx` (kebab-case)
- `safarButton.tsx` (camelCase)
- `Button.tsx` without Safari prefix for branded components

**Why:** Consistency across codebase, easy identification of Safari-branded vs generic components

**Source:** [best-practices/ui-design.md](~/.claude/learning/best-practices/ui-design.md)
```

---

*This pipeline ensures learning doesn't stay as documentation - it becomes enforced behavior.*

1→# Sync Testing Guide
     2→
     3→Complete guide to verify that your Claude Code setup is synchronized across computers.
     4→
     5→## Quick Test (Home Computer - Current)
     6→
     7→### Step 1: Verify Local Setup
     8→
     9→```powershell
    10→# Run verification script
    11→& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\scripts\verify-sync.ps1"
    12→
    13→# Or with detailed output
    14→& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\scripts\verify-sync.ps1" -Detailed
    15→```
    16→
    17→**Expected Result:** All checks pass (green ✓)
    18→
    19→### Step 2: Test Commands in Claude Code
    20→
    21→In this Claude Code session, try:
    22→
    23→```
    24→/pulse
    25→/learn Test learning capture
    26→/status
    27→/checkpoint
    28→```
    29→
    30→**Expected Result:** All commands work without errors
    31→
    32→### Step 3: Commit and Push Changes
    33→
    34→```powershell
    35→cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
    36→git add .
    37→git commit -m "Add enterprise commands, agents, and learning structure"
    38→git push
    39→```
    40→
    41→**Expected Result:** Changes pushed to GitHub successfully
    42→
    43→## Full Test (Work Computer - After Sync)
    44→
    45→### Step 1: Pull Latest Changes
    46→
    47→```powershell
    48→cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
    49→git pull
    50→```
    51→
    52→**Expected Result:** New files downloaded (commands, agents, scripts)
    53→
    54→### Step 2: Verify Setup
    55→
    56→```powershell
    57→& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\scripts\verify-sync.ps1" -Detailed
    58→```
    59→
    60→**Expected Result:** All checks pass, same command count as home computer
    61→
    62→### Step 3: Test Commands
    63→
    64→Start a new Claude Code session and try the same commands:
    65→
    66→```
    67→/pulse
    68→/learn Testing sync between computers
    69→/status
    70→```
    71→
    72→**Expected Result:** Commands work identically to home computer
    73→
    74→### Step 4: Test Sync Back
    75→
    76→Make a change on work computer:
    77→
    78→```powershell
    79→cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
    80→# Add a test file
    81→echo "Test from work computer" > .claude\enterprise\test-sync.txt
    82→git add .
    83→git commit -m "Test sync from work computer"
    84→git push
    85→```
    86→
    87→Then on home computer:
    88→
    89→```powershell
    90→cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
    91→git pull
    92→Get-Content .claude\enterprise\test-sync.txt
    93→```
    94→
    95→**Expected Result:** File appears on home computer with correct content
    96→
    97→## Verification Checklist
    98→
    99→### Home Computer (eduar)
   100→
   101→- [ ] personal-claude-docs repo exists at `C:\Users\eduar\personal-claude-docs`
   102→- [ ] Symlink `~\.claude\enterprise` → `personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise`
   103→- [ ] 13+ commands available in `~\.claude\enterprise\commands`
   104→- [ ] 3+ agents available in `~\.claude\enterprise\agents`
   105→- [ ] Learning structure exists
   106→- [ ] Commands work in Claude Code session
   107→- [ ] Git status clean (or committed)
   108→- [ ] Changes pushed to GitHub
   109→
   110→### Work Computer (emarquez)
   111→
   112→- [ ] personal-claude-docs repo exists at `C:\Users\emarquez\personal-claude-docs`
   113→- [ ] Symlink `~\.claude\enterprise` → `personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise`
   114→- [ ] Same command count as home computer
   115→- [ ] Same agent count as home computer
   116→- [ ] Commands work in Claude Code session
   117→- [ ] Git pull brings latest changes
   118→- [ ] Test push/pull cycle completes
   119→
   120→## Troubleshooting
   121→
   122→### Commands don't work after pull
   123→
   124→**Issue:** Commands show in file system but not available in Claude Code
   125→
   126→**Fix:** Restart Claude Code or start a new session
   127→
   128→### Symlinks broken after pull
   129→
   130→**Issue:** `verify-sync.ps1` shows symlink errors
   131→
   132→**Fix:** Re-run setup script
   133→```powershell
   134→& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\scripts\setup-personal.ps1"
   135→```
   136→
   137→### Git conflicts on pull
   138→
   139→**Issue:** Merge conflicts when pulling changes
   140→
   141→**Fix:** 
   142→```powershell
   143→git status
   144→# Review conflicts
   145→git merge --abort  # if needed
   146→git pull --rebase  # alternative approach
   147→```
   148→
   149→### Different file counts between computers
   150→
   151→**Issue:** Home has 13 commands, work has 8 commands
   152→
   153→**Fix:** Ensure both computers pulled latest changes
   154→```powershell
   155→cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
   156→git fetch --all
   157→git status  # Check if behind origin
   158→git pull
   159→```
   160→
   161→## Expected File Structure
   162→
   163→After successful sync, both computers should have:
   164→
   165→```
   166→~/.claude/
   167→├── enterprise/ (symlink → personal-claude-docs/.claude/enterprise/)
   168→│   ├── commands/
   169→│   │   ├── brain-dump.md
   170→│   │   ├── checkpoint.md

file.content:
# Sync Testing Guide

Complete guide to verify that your Claude Code setup is synchronized across computers.

## Quick Test (Home Computer - Current)

### Step 1: Verify Local Setup

```powershell
# Run verification script
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\scripts\verify-sync.ps1"

# Or with detailed output
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\scripts\verify-sync.ps1" -Detailed
```

**Expected Result:** All checks pass (green ✓)

### Step 2: Test Commands in Claude Code

In this Claude Code session, try:

```
/pulse
/learn Test learning capture
/status
/checkpoint
```

**Expected Result:** All commands work without errors

### Step 3: Commit and Push Changes

```powershell
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git add .
git commit -m "Add enterprise commands, agents, and learning structure"
git push
```

**Expected Result:** Changes pushed to GitHub successfully

## Full Test (Work Computer - After Sync)

### Step 1: Pull Latest Changes

```powershell
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git pull
```

**Expected Result:** New files downloaded (commands, agents, scripts)

### Step 2: Verify Setup

```powershell
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\scripts\verify-sync.ps1" -Detailed
```

**Expected Result:** All checks pass, same command count as home computer

### Step 3: Test Commands

Start a new Claude Code session and try the same commands:

```
/pulse
/learn Testing sync between computers
/status
```

**Expected Result:** Commands work identically to home computer

### Step 4: Test Sync Back

Make a change on work computer:

```powershell
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
# Add a test file
echo "Test from work computer" > .claude\enterprise\test-sync.txt
git add .
git commit -m "Test sync from work computer"
git push
```

Then on home computer:

```powershell
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git pull
Get-Content .claude\enterprise\test-sync.txt
```

**Expected Result:** File appears on home computer with correct content

## Verification Checklist

### Home Computer (eduar)

- [ ] personal-claude-docs repo exists at `C:\Users\eduar\personal-claude-docs`
- [ ] Symlink `~\.claude\enterprise` → `personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise`
- [ ] 13+ commands available in `~\.claude\enterprise\commands`
- [ ] 3+ agents available in `~\.claude\enterprise\agents`
- [ ] Learning structure exists
- [ ] Commands work in Claude Code session
- [ ] Git status clean (or committed)
- [ ] Changes pushed to GitHub

### Work Computer (emarquez)

- [ ] personal-claude-docs repo exists at `C:\Users\emarquez\personal-claude-docs`
- [ ] Symlink `~\.claude\enterprise` → `personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise`
- [ ] Same command count as home computer
- [ ] Same agent count as home computer
- [ ] Commands work in Claude Code session
- [ ] Git pull brings latest changes
- [ ] Test push/pull cycle completes

## Troubleshooting

### Commands don't work after pull

**Issue:** Commands show in file system but not available in Claude Code

**Fix:** Restart Claude Code or start a new session

### Symlinks broken after pull

**Issue:** `verify-sync.ps1` shows symlink errors

**Fix:** Re-run setup script
```powershell
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\scripts\setup-personal.ps1"
```

### Git conflicts on pull

**Issue:** Merge conflicts when pulling changes

**Fix:** 
```powershell
git status
# Review conflicts
git merge --abort  # if needed
git pull --rebase  # alternative approach
```

### Different file counts between computers

**Issue:** Home has 13 commands, work has 8 commands

**Fix:** Ensure both computers pulled latest changes
```powershell
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git fetch --all
git status  # Check if behind origin
git pull
```

## Expected File Structure

After successful sync, both computers should have:

```
~/.claude/
├── enterprise/ (symlink → personal-claude-docs/.claude/enterprise/)
│   ├── commands/
│   │   ├── brain-dump.md
│   │   ├── checkpoint.md
│   │   ├── end-work.md
│   │   ├── implement.md
│   │   ├── learn.md
│   │   ├── plan.md
│   │   ├── pulse.md
│   │   ├── quick-commit.md
│   │   ├── README.md
│   │   ├── reminder.md
│   │   ├── research.md
│   │   ├── start-work.md
│   │   ├── status.md
│   │   └── validate-work.md
│   ├── agents/
│   │   ├── codebase-analyzer.md
│   │   ├── codebase-locator.md
│   │   ├── pattern-finder.md
│   │   └── README.md
│   ├── learning/
│   │   ├── ideas/
│   │   │   ├── backlog.md
│   │   │   └── lifecycle.md
│   │   ├── best-practices/
│   │   └── PIPELINE.md
│   ├── scripts/
│   │   ├── verify-sync.ps1
│   │   └── SYNC-TEST-GUIDE.md
│   ├── CLAUDE.md
│   └── boris-workflow.md
└── personal/ (symlink → personal-claude-docs/.claude/users/emarquez-personal/)
    ├── CLAUDE.md
    └── ...
```

## Sync Workflow

### Daily Work Pattern

**Home Computer:**
```powershell
# Morning
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git pull  # Get latest from work

# Work...

# Evening
git add .
git commit -m "Description of changes"
git push
```

**Work Computer:**
```powershell
# Morning
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git pull  # Get latest from home

# Work...

# Evening  
git add .
git commit -m "Description of changes"
git push
```

### When to Commit

**Commit after:**
- Adding new commands or agents
- Creating new learnings or memories
- Updating CLAUDE.md
- Discovering new patterns to document

**Don't commit:**
- Session-specific state
- Temporary test files
- Personal API keys or secrets
- Computer-specific configuration (those go in machine-specific CLAUDE.md)

## Validation Commands

Quick one-liners to verify sync:

```powershell
# Check command count
(Get-ChildItem "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\enterprise\commands\*.md" | Where-Object { $_.Name -ne "README.md" }).Count

# Check agent count
(Get-ChildItem "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\enterprise\agents\*.md" | Where-Object { $_.Name -ne "README.md" }).Count

# Check git status
Push-Location "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs"; git status; Pop-Location

# Check if behind origin
Push-Location "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs"; git fetch; git status; Pop-Location

# List all commands
Get-ChildItem "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\enterprise\commands\*.md" | Where-Object { $_.Name -ne "README.md" } | Select-Object -ExpandProperty BaseName | ForEach-Object { "/$_" }
```

## Success Criteria

✅ Sync is working correctly when:

1. **Verification script passes** on both computers
2. **Same file counts** for commands and agents
3. **Commands work identically** on both computers
4. **Git pull** brings changes from other computer
5. **Git push** sends changes to other computer
6. **No symlink errors** in verification
7. **No git conflicts** on routine pulls

## Maintenance

**Weekly:** Run verification script on both computers
**After major changes:** Test sync cycle (push from one, pull on other)
**If issues:** Check symlinks, re-run setup script if needed

## Related Documentation

- `personal-claude-docs/.claude/enterprise/CLAUDE.md` - Enterprise configuration
- `personal-claude-docs/.claude/users/emarquez-personal/CLAUDE.md` - Personal preferences
- `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` - Machine-specific loader (loads above files)

input.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

---

## Repository Purpose

**personal-claude-docs** (aka ClaudeDocs) is a shared Claude Code knowledge base synchronized across multiple computers via Git. It contains:

- Enterprise configuration (workflow, principles, patterns)
- Custom commands (`/status`, `/plan`, `/implement`, etc.)
- Reusable skills and agents
- Learning pipeline (ideas → best practices → enforced standards)
- Personal preferences and tech stack

This repo is **NOT** a project to build software. It's **infrastructure** that wires Claude Code to work consistently across home, work, and laptop computers.

---

## Architecture Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ personal-claude-docs (this repo)                                │
│   └── .claude/enterprise/   ← Shared config (git-synced)       │
│   └── .claude/users/         ← Personal prefs (git-synced)     │
│   └── .claude/scripts/       ← Setup and verification tools    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              │
                              │ Symlinked by setup-personal.ps1
                              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ~/.claude/ (machine-specific repo: ClaudeHome or ClaudeWork)    │
│   commands/    ──symlink──► personal-claude-docs/enterprise/commands
│   skills/      ──symlink──► personal-claude-docs/enterprise/skills
│   learning/    ──symlink──► personal-claude-docs/enterprise/learning
│   agents/      ──symlink──► personal-claude-docs/enterprise/agents
│   reference/   ──symlink──► personal-claude-docs/enterprise/reference
│   enterprise/  ──symlink──► personal-claude-docs/enterprise/
│   personal/    ──symlink──► personal-claude-docs/users/emarquez-personal/
│   CLAUDE.md    ← Auto-generated loader (@enterprise + @personal)
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              │
                              │ Mirrored via symlinks (for Obsidian editing)
                              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\{vault}/   (01_Claude_Home or 00_Claude) │
│   04-Commands/_claude-commands   ──symlink──► ~/.claude/commands
│   03-Skills/_claude-skills       ──symlink──► ~/.claude/skills
│   02-Learnings/_claude-learning  ──symlink──► ~/.claude/learning
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Three-layer sync:**
1. **Git** (primary): personal-claude-docs → GitHub → other computers
2. **Symlinks** (runtime): personal-claude-docs → ~/.claude/ (Claude Code reads here)
3. **Obsidian** (editing): ~/.claude/ → vault (optional, for visual editing)

**Key invariant:** Git is the source of truth. OneDrive mirrors are read-only for Claude.

---

## Setup and Maintenance

### Initial Setup on New Machine

```powershell
# 1. Clone this repo
git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeDocs.git "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs"

# 2. Run setup (auto-detects home vs work based on username)
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\scripts\setup-personal.ps1"

# 3. Verify setup
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\scripts\verify-sync.ps1" -Detailed
```

**What setup-personal.ps1 does:**
- Detects computer profile (home: `eduar`, work: `emarquez`, laptop: auto)
- Creates symlinks from `~/.claude/` → `personal-claude-docs/.claude/enterprise/`
- Generates `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` with `@enterprise` and `@personal` imports
- Links project memory to enable git-based sync of session state
- (Work only) Wires Safari Circuits organizational standards via `@safari` layer
- Falls back to junctions if admin rights unavailable
- Adds PowerShell profile shortcut (`claude-session` / `cs`)

### Daily Sync Workflow

```powershell
# Morning: Pull latest changes from other computer
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git pull

# During work: Claude Code uses symlinked content automatically

# Evening: Commit and push changes
git add .claude/enterprise/ .claude/users/
git commit -m "Learn: [what you discovered]"
git push
```

**No restart needed after pull** — symlinks keep `~/.claude/` pointing to latest content.

### Verification

```powershell
# Quick check
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\scripts\verify-sync.ps1"

# Detailed report (command count, symlink targets, git status)
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\scripts\verify-sync.ps1" -Detailed

# Manual check
Get-Item ~/.claude/commands
# Should show: Mode: l----- (symlink) Target: ...\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\commands
```

**Success criteria:**
- All symlinks resolve
- Command count matches across computers
- Git status shows latest commit from other machine after pull

See `.claude/enterprise/scripts/SYNC-TEST-GUIDE.md` for comprehensive testing procedures.

---

## Adding and Modifying Content

### Adding a Custom Command

```powershell
# 1. Create file in enterprise/commands/
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
New-Item .claude\enterprise\commands\my-command.md

# 2. Edit with standard format:
#    - Description
#    - Parameters
#    - Implementation
#    - Examples

# 3. Commit and push
git add .claude/enterprise/commands/my-command.md
git commit -m "Add /my-command for [purpose]"
git push

# 4. Use immediately in new session
# (Restart Claude Code or `/clear` to reload commands)
/my-command
```

**Command format:** See existing commands in `.claude/enterprise/commands/` for templates.

### Adding a Skill

```powershell
# 1. Create skill directory
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
New-Item -ItemType Directory .claude\enterprise\skills\my-skill

# 2. Create SKILL.md with skill definition
# See .claude/enterprise/skills/example-skill/SKILL.md for template

# 3. Commit
git add .claude/enterprise/skills/my-skill/
git commit -m "Add my-skill for [purpose]"
git push
```

### Capturing Learnings

**Quick capture:**
```powershell
# Append to ideas backlog (unvalidated notes)
Add-Content .claude\enterprise\learning\ideas\backlog.md "`n- [$(Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-dd')] Learning: [insight]"
git add .claude/enterprise/learning/ideas/backlog.md
git commit -m "Learn: [insight]"
git push
```

**Promotion pipeline:**
1. **Idea** → `.claude/enterprise/learning/ideas/backlog.md`
2. **Validated** → `.claude/enterprise/learning/best-practices/{topic}.md`
3. **Standard** → `.claude/enterprise/CLAUDE.md` (enforced by Claude)

See `.claude/enterprise/learning/PIPELINE.md` for full promotion criteria.

### Modifying Configuration

**Enterprise config (shared across all computers):**
```powershell
# Edit the master config
notepad $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\CLAUDE.md

# Commit
git add .claude/enterprise/CLAUDE.md
git commit -m "Update enterprise config: [what changed]"
git push
```

**Personal config (user preferences, tech stack):**
```powershell
# Edit personal layer
notepad $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\users\emarquez-personal\CLAUDE.md

# Commit
git add .claude/users/emarquez-personal/
git commit -m "Update personal prefs: [what changed]"
git push
```

**Machine-specific config (local paths only):**
```powershell
# This is auto-generated by setup-personal.ps1
# To regenerate:
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\scripts\setup-personal.ps1" -Force
```

---

## Key Files and Directories

| Path | Purpose | Sync Method |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `.claude/enterprise/CLAUDE.md` | Core workflow, principles, patterns | Git |
| `.claude/enterprise/commands/` | Custom `/commands` | Git |
| `.claude/enterprise/skills/` | Reusable workflows | Git |
| `.claude/enterprise/learning/` | Knowledge capture pipeline | Git |
| `.claude/enterprise/agents/` | Subagent definitions | Git |
| `.claude/enterprise/scripts/` | Setup and verification tools | Git |
| `.claude/users/emarquez-personal/CLAUDE.md` | Personal preferences | Git |
| `.claude/scripts/setup-personal.ps1` | Bootstrap script (called by machine-specific setup) | Git |
| `.claude/project-memory/` | Session state and project memory | Git (NEW: syncs across machines) |

**Do NOT commit to this repo:**
- Workspace-specific files from other projects
- API keys, credentials, secrets
- Large binary files
- Temporary test files

---

## Troubleshooting

### Symlinks Broken

**Symptom:** Commands don't work, verification fails

**Fix:**
```powershell
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\scripts\setup-personal.ps1" -Force
```

### Git Conflicts on Pull

**Symptom:** Merge conflicts when pulling changes

**Fix:**
```powershell
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git status          # Review conflicts
git stash           # Stash local changes if needed
git pull            # Pull clean
git stash pop       # Re-apply local changes
# Resolve conflicts manually, then:
git add .
git commit -m "Merge: resolve conflicts"
git push
```

### Different File Counts Between Computers

**Symptom:** Home has 13 commands, work has 8

**Fix:**
```powershell
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git fetch --all
git status  # Check if behind origin
git pull
```

### Commands Not Available After Pull

**Symptom:** Files exist but commands don't work in Claude Code

**Fix:** Restart Claude Code session or use `/clear` to reload

---

## Architecture Decisions

### Why Symlinks Instead of Git Clones?

**Problem:** Multiple repos (ClaudeHome, ClaudeWork, ClaudeDocs) would drift out of sync.

**Solution:** One shared repo (ClaudeDocs) symlinked into each machine's `~/.claude/`.
- Single source of truth
- Instant sync (no re-clone needed)
- Works with Obsidian vaults simultaneously

### Why Three Layers (Git + Symlinks + OneDrive)?

**Git:** Source of truth, cross-machine sync, version control
**Symlinks:** Runtime wiring (Claude Code reads from `~/.claude/`)
**OneDrive:** Optional visual editing in Obsidian, automatic backup

Each layer has a specific purpose and doesn't duplicate the others.

### Why Separate Enterprise and Personal Layers?

**Enterprise:** Shared workflow practices (Boris Cherny methodology, RPI pattern)
**Personal:** Tech stack, communication style, current focus areas
**Work:** Safari Circuits organizational standards (work computer only)

Separation allows:
- Reuse enterprise layer for other users/teams
- Customize personal prefs without affecting workflow
- Keep work context isolated to work machine

### Project Memory Sync (New)

Previously, `~/.claude/projects/` (project memory) was local-only.
Now it's symlinked to `.claude/project-memory/` and synced via Git.

**Benefit:** Session state and learnings follow you across machines.
**Trade-off:** Must manually resolve conflicts if working on same project from both computers simultaneously.

---

## Work Computer Integration (Safari Circuits)

```powershell
~/.claude/safari/              → organizational-docs/.claude/
~/.claude/reference/safari/    → organizational-docs/shared/
~/.claude/CLAUDE.md includes   → @safari/CLAUDE.md
```

This wires organizational standards (branding, APIs, security policies) into Claude sessions.

**Requirement:** `organizational-docs` repo must exist at `~/organizational-docs`.

---

## References

toolUseResult.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

---

## Repository Purpose

**personal-claude-docs** (aka ClaudeDocs) is a shared Claude Code knowledge base synchronized across multiple computers via Git. It contains:

- Enterprise configuration (workflow, principles, patterns)
- Custom commands (`/status`, `/plan`, `/implement`, etc.)
- Reusable skills and agents
- Learning pipeline (ideas → best practices → enforced standards)
- Personal preferences and tech stack

This repo is **NOT** a project to build software. It's **infrastructure** that wires Claude Code to work consistently across home, work, and laptop computers.

---

## Architecture Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ personal-claude-docs (this repo)                                │
│   └── .claude/enterprise/   ← Shared config (git-synced)       │
│   └── .claude/users/         ← Personal prefs (git-synced)     │
│   └── .claude/scripts/       ← Setup and verification tools    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              │
                              │ Symlinked by setup-personal.ps1
                              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ~/.claude/ (machine-specific repo: ClaudeHome or ClaudeWork)    │
│   commands/    ──symlink──► personal-claude-docs/enterprise/commands
│   skills/      ──symlink──► personal-claude-docs/enterprise/skills
│   learning/    ──symlink──► personal-claude-docs/enterprise/learning
│   agents/      ──symlink──► personal-claude-docs/enterprise/agents
│   reference/   ──symlink──► personal-claude-docs/enterprise/reference
│   enterprise/  ──symlink──► personal-claude-docs/enterprise/
│   personal/    ──symlink──► personal-claude-docs/users/emarquez-personal/
│   CLAUDE.md    ← Auto-generated loader (@enterprise + @personal)
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              │
                              │ Mirrored via symlinks (for Obsidian editing)
                              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\{vault}/   (01_Claude_Home or 00_Claude) │
│   04-Commands/_claude-commands   ──symlink──► ~/.claude/commands
│   03-Skills/_claude-skills       ──symlink──► ~/.claude/skills
│   02-Learnings/_claude-learning  ──symlink──► ~/.claude/learning
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Three-layer sync:**
1. **Git** (primary): personal-claude-docs → GitHub → other computers
2. **Symlinks** (runtime): personal-claude-docs → ~/.claude/ (Claude Code reads here)
3. **Obsidian** (editing): ~/.claude/ → vault (optional, for visual editing)

**Key invariant:** Git is the source of truth. OneDrive mirrors are read-only for Claude.

---

## Setup and Maintenance

### Initial Setup on New Machine

```powershell
# 1. Clone this repo
git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeDocs.git "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs"

# 2. Run setup (auto-detects home vs work based on username)
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\scripts\setup-personal.ps1"

# 3. Verify setup
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\scripts\verify-sync.ps1" -Detailed
```

**What setup-personal.ps1 does:**
- Detects computer profile (home: `eduar`, work: `emarquez`, laptop: auto)
- Creates symlinks from `~/.claude/` → `personal-claude-docs/.claude/enterprise/`
- Generates `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` with `@enterprise` and `@personal` imports
- Links project memory to enable git-based sync of session state
- (Work only) Wires Safari Circuits organizational standards via `@safari` layer
- Falls back to junctions if admin rights unavailable
- Adds PowerShell profile shortcut (`claude-session` / `cs`)

### Daily Sync Workflow

```powershell
# Morning: Pull latest changes from other computer
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git pull

# During work: Claude Code uses symlinked content automatically

# Evening: Commit and push changes
git add .claude/enterprise/ .claude/users/
git commit -m "Learn: [what you discovered]"
git push
```

**No restart needed after pull** — symlinks keep `~/.claude/` pointing to latest content.

### Verification

```powershell
# Quick check
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\scripts\verify-sync.ps1"

# Detailed report (command count, symlink targets, git status)
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\scripts\verify-sync.ps1" -Detailed

# Manual check
Get-Item ~/.claude/commands
# Should show: Mode: l----- (symlink) Target: ...\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\commands
```

**Success criteria:**
- All symlinks resolve
- Command count matches across computers
- Git status shows latest commit from other machine after pull

See `.claude/enterprise/scripts/SYNC-TEST-GUIDE.md` for comprehensive testing procedures.

---

## Adding and Modifying Content

### Adding a Custom Command

```powershell
# 1. Create file in enterprise/commands/
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
New-Item .claude\enterprise\commands\my-command.md

# 2. Edit with standard format:
#    - Description
#    - Parameters
#    - Implementation
#    - Examples

# 3. Commit and push
git add .claude/enterprise/commands/my-command.md
git commit -m "Add /my-command for [purpose]"
git push

# 4. Use immediately in new session
# (Restart Claude Code or `/clear` to reload commands)
/my-command
```

**Command format:** See existing commands in `.claude/enterprise/commands/` for templates.

### Adding a Skill

```powershell
# 1. Create skill directory
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
New-Item -ItemType Directory .claude\enterprise\skills\my-skill

# 2. Create SKILL.md with skill definition
# See .claude/enterprise/skills/example-skill/SKILL.md for template

# 3. Commit
git add .claude/enterprise/skills/my-skill/
git commit -m "Add my-skill for [purpose]"
git push
```

### Capturing Learnings

**Quick capture:**
```powershell
# Append to ideas backlog (unvalidated notes)
Add-Content .claude\enterprise\learning\ideas\backlog.md "`n- [$(Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-dd')] Learning: [insight]"
git add .claude/enterprise/learning/ideas/backlog.md
git commit -m "Learn: [insight]"
git push
```

**Promotion pipeline:**
1. **Idea** → `.claude/enterprise/learning/ideas/backlog.md`
2. **Validated** → `.claude/enterprise/learning/best-practices/{topic}.md`
3. **Standard** → `.claude/enterprise/CLAUDE.md` (enforced by Claude)

See `.claude/enterprise/learning/PIPELINE.md` for full promotion criteria.

### Modifying Configuration

**Enterprise config (shared across all computers):**
```powershell
# Edit the master config
notepad $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\CLAUDE.md

# Commit
git add .claude/enterprise/CLAUDE.md
git commit -m "Update enterprise config: [what changed]"
git push
```

**Personal config (user preferences, tech stack):**
```powershell
# Edit personal layer
notepad $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\users\emarquez-personal\CLAUDE.md

# Commit
git add .claude/users/emarquez-personal/
git commit -m "Update personal prefs: [what changed]"
git push
```

**Machine-specific config (local paths only):**
```powershell
# This is auto-generated by setup-personal.ps1
# To regenerate:
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\scripts\setup-personal.ps1" -Force
```

---

## Key Files and Directories

| Path | Purpose | Sync Method |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `.claude/enterprise/CLAUDE.md` | Core workflow, principles, patterns | Git |
| `.claude/enterprise/commands/` | Custom `/commands` | Git |
| `.claude/enterprise/skills/` | Reusable workflows | Git |
| `.claude/enterprise/learning/` | Knowledge capture pipeline | Git |
| `.claude/enterprise/agents/` | Subagent definitions | Git |
| `.claude/enterprise/scripts/` | Setup and verification tools | Git |
| `.claude/users/emarquez-personal/CLAUDE.md` | Personal preferences | Git |
| `.claude/scripts/setup-personal.ps1` | Bootstrap script (called by machine-specific setup) | Git |
| `.claude/project-memory/` | Session state and project memory | Git (NEW: syncs across machines) |

**Do NOT commit to this repo:**
- Workspace-specific files from other projects
- API keys, credentials, secrets
- Large binary files
- Temporary test files

---

## Troubleshooting

### Symlinks Broken

**Symptom:** Commands don't work, verification fails

**Fix:**
```powershell
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\scripts\setup-personal.ps1" -Force
```

### Git Conflicts on Pull

**Symptom:** Merge conflicts when pulling changes

**Fix:**
```powershell
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git status          # Review conflicts
git stash           # Stash local changes if needed
git pull            # Pull clean
git stash pop       # Re-apply local changes
# Resolve conflicts manually, then:
git add .
git commit -m "Merge: resolve conflicts"
git push
```

### Different File Counts Between Computers

**Symptom:** Home has 13 commands, work has 8

**Fix:**
```powershell
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git fetch --all
git status  # Check if behind origin
git pull
```

### Commands Not Available After Pull

**Symptom:** Files exist but commands don't work in Claude Code

**Fix:** Restart Claude Code session or use `/clear` to reload

---

## Architecture Decisions

### Why Symlinks Instead of Git Clones?

**Problem:** Multiple repos (ClaudeHome, ClaudeWork, ClaudeDocs) would drift out of sync.

**Solution:** One shared repo (ClaudeDocs) symlinked into each machine's `~/.claude/`.
- Single source of truth
- Instant sync (no re-clone needed)
- Works with Obsidian vaults simultaneously

### Why Three Layers (Git + Symlinks + OneDrive)?

**Git:** Source of truth, cross-machine sync, version control
**Symlinks:** Runtime wiring (Claude Code reads from `~/.claude/`)
**OneDrive:** Optional visual editing in Obsidian, automatic backup

Each layer has a specific purpose and doesn't duplicate the others.

### Why Separate Enterprise and Personal Layers?

**Enterprise:** Shared workflow practices (Boris Cherny methodology, RPI pattern)
**Personal:** Tech stack, communication style, current focus areas
**Work:** Safari Circuits organizational standards (work computer only)

Separation allows:
- Reuse enterprise layer for other users/teams
- Customize personal prefs without affecting workflow
- Keep work context isolated to work machine

### Project Memory Sync (New)

Previously, `~/.claude/projects/` (project memory) was local-only.
Now it's symlinked to `.claude/project-memory/` and synced via Git.

**Benefit:** Session state and learnings follow you across machines.
**Trade-off:** Must manually resolve conflicts if working on same project from both computers simultaneously.

---

## Work Computer Integration (Safari Circuits)

On work computer (`emarquez` user), setup-personal.ps1 adds:

```powershell
~/.claude/safari/              → organizational-docs/.claude/
~/.claude/reference/safari/    → organizational-docs/shared/
~/.claude/CLAUDE.md includes   → @safari/CLAUDE.md
```

This wires organizational standards (branding, APIs, security policies) into Claude sessions.

**Requirement:** `organizational-docs` repo must exist at `~/organizational-docs`.

---

## References

- **Setup script:** `.claude/scripts/setup-personal.ps1`
- **Verification:** `.claude/enterprise/scripts/verify-sync.ps1`
- **Testing guide:** `.claude/enterprise/scripts/SYNC-TEST-GUIDE.md`
- **Learning pipeline:** `.claude/enterprise/learning/PIPELINE.md`
- **Enterprise config:** `.claude/enterprise/CLAUDE.md`
- **Personal config:** `.claude/users/emarquez-personal/CLAUDE.md`

**External sources:**
- [Boris Cherny's Claude Code workflow](https://x.com/bcherny/status/2007179832300581177)
- [Advanced Context Engineering](https://github.com/humanlayer/advanced-context-engineering-for-coding-agents)
- [12-Factor Agents](https://github.com/humanlayer/12-factor-agents)

content.text:
Great question! Based on your current architecture, here's the best approach for Safari Circuits company projects:

## Recommended Structure

```
S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\
└── Projects\                          ← New: Company project workspace
    ├── CMMC-Level-2\
    │   ├── CLAUDE.md                 ← Project context, goals, key files
    │   ├── research\                 ← Documents, PDFs, notes
    │   ├── presentations\            ← Generated outputs
    │   └── requirements.md
    ├── Azure-Migration\
    │   ├── CLAUDE.md
    │   └── ...
    └── BeFirst-Modernization\
        ├── CLAUDE.md
        └── ...
```

## How It Works

### 1. Create Project in OneDrive

```powershell
# Create project directory
$projectPath = "S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Projects\CMMC-Level-2"
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $projectPath -Force
cd $projectPath

# Initialize with CLAUDE.md
@"
# CMMC Level 2 Compliance Project

## Project Overview
Goal: Achieve CMMC Level 2 certification for Safari Circuits
Timeline: Q2-Q3 2026
Stakeholders: Scott (IT Director), Eduardo (IT Manager), External auditor

## Key Focus Areas
- Access control (AC)
- Incident response (IR)
- System and information integrity (SI)
- Security assessment (CA)

## Resources
- Requirements: requirements.md
- Current gap analysis: research/gap-analysis.xlsx
- Remediation plan: research/remediation-plan.md

## Quick Commands
- Review gap analysis: Read research/gap-analysis.xlsx
- Generate presentation: "Create executive summary presentation for CMMC progress"
- Update checklist: Edit requirements.md

## Related OneDrive Folders
- Policies: S:\...\Information Technology - Documents\Policies\Security
- Audit reports: S:\...\Information Technology - Documents\Compliance\Audits
"@ | Out-File "$projectPath\CLAUDE.md" -Encoding utf8
```

### 2. Work in That Directory

```powershell
# Navigate to project
cd "S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Projects\CMMC-Level-2"

# Start Claude Code session
claude
```

**What happens:**
- Claude automatically loads `CLAUDE.md` from the current directory
- Project-specific context becomes active
- Memory accumulates at `~/.claude/projects/S--OneDrive...-CMMC-Level-2/memory/`

### 3. Knowledge Base Builds Automatically

As you work on the project:
```
User: "What are the AC.1 requirements?"
Claude: [Answers based on documents you've shared]

User: "Create a presentation for Scott summarizing our progress"
Claude: [Generates presentation]

User: "Remember that we decided to use Azure AD for access control"
Claude: [Stores in project memory]
```

**Next time you open this project:**
```powershell
cd "S:\...\Projects\CMMC-Level-2"
claude
```

Claude automatically remembers:
- Project context from CLAUDE.md
- Decisions and discussions from memory
- File locations and structure

## Integration with Your Current Setup

### Option A: Keep It Simple (Recommended for Start)

Just use OneDrive + project memory:
- ✅ Projects live in company OneDrive (already backed up, accessible)
- ✅ CLAUDE.md per project (context always loaded)
- ✅ Project memory auto-syncs via personal-claude-docs (since you symlinked it)
- ✅ No additional repos to manage

### Option B: Add Projects Index to organizational-docs

If you want centralized tracking:

```powershell
# In organizational-docs repo
$indexPath = "$env:USERPROFILE\organizational-docs\.claude\projects-index.md"

@"
# Safari Circuits Projects Index

| Project | Status | Location | Started |
|---------|--------|----------|---------|
| CMMC Level 2 | Active | S:\OneDrive...\Projects\CMMC-Level-2 | 2026-05-11 |
| Azure Migration | Planning | S:\OneDrive...\Projects\Azure-Migration | 2026-04-15 |
| BeFirst Modernization | Active | S:\OneDrive...\Projects\BeFirst-Modernization | 2026-03-01 |

## Quick Access

\`\`\`powershell
# CMMC Level 2
cd "S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Projects\CMMC-Level-2"

# Azure Migration  
cd "S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Projects\Azure-Migration"
\`\`\`
"@ | Out-File $indexPath -Encoding utf8
```

Then reference it in your work CLAUDE.md:
```markdown
## Safari Circuits Projects

See active projects: @safari/projects-index.md
```

## Project CLAUDE.md Template

Create a template for new projects:

```powershell
# Save as: organizational-docs/templates/project-claude-template.md
@"
# {Project Name}

## Project Overview
**Goal:** {What you're trying to achieve}
**Timeline:** {Start - End}
**Stakeholders:** {Key people}
**Status:** {Planning | Active | On Hold | Complete}

## Context for Claude
{Brief description of what this project is about, key terminology, relevant background}

## Key Focus Areas
- {Area 1}
- {Area 2}
- {Area 3}

## Resources
- {Important files, their locations, and what they contain}
- {External references, URLs, documentation}

## Frequent Tasks
- {Common thing you do}: {How to do it or where to find info}
- {Another common task}: {Instructions}

## Related Locations
- {Related OneDrive folders}
- {Related SharePoint sites}
- {Related repos}

## Decisions Log
*Use /learn to capture important decisions as you make them*

- [YYYY-MM-DD] Decision: {What was decided and why}
"@
```

## Usage Workflow

### Starting a New Project

```powershell
# 1. Create project directory
$projectName = "CMMC-Level-2"
$projectPath = "S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Projects\$projectName"
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $projectPath -Force

# 2. Copy template
Copy-Item "$env:USERPROFILE\organizational-docs\templates\project-claude-template.md" `
          "$projectPath\CLAUDE.md"

# 3. Edit CLAUDE.md with project specifics
code "$projectPath\CLAUDE.md"

# 4. Start working
cd $projectPath
claude
```

### Daily Work

```powershell
# Just navigate and start
cd "S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Projects\CMMC-Level-2"
claude

# Or use a PowerShell alias
function cmmc { cd "S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Projects\CMMC-Level-2"; claude }
```

### Capturing Knowledge

```
User: "/learn We decided to implement MFA using Microsoft Authenticator because it's already deployed company-wide"

Claude: [Captures to project memory + optionally to CLAUDE.md decisions log]

User: "Create a summary of our access control decisions"

Claude: [Pulls from accumulated project memory to generate summary]
```

## Why This Approach?

| ✅ Benefits | How It Helps |
|------------|--------------|
| **OneDrive native** | Already backed up, version history, accessible from anywhere |
| **Project memory auto-syncs** | Your symlink setup means memory follows you home → work → home |
| **No extra repos** | Don't need separate git repos for each project |
| **Context switching** | Just `cd` to project, Claude loads everything automatically |
| **Company knowledge base** | Memory accumulates, decisions are preserved |
| **Presentations/outputs stay with project** | Generated files live next to source materials |

## Summary

**Recommended path:**

1. **Create:** `S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Projects\{project-name}\`
2. **Add:** `CLAUDE.md` in each project directory with context, goals, resources
3. **Work:** `cd` to project directory, start Claude Code
4. **Knowledge builds:** Project memory accumulates automatically, syncs via your personal-claude-docs setup
5. **Optional:** Add projects index to organizational-docs for central tracking

This fits perfectly with your current architecture and leverages:
- OneDrive (company backup/sync)

│ 6. IT SUPPORT / HELP DESK        │ ✓ INCLUDED          │ ✗ NOT INCLUDED      │ ✓ INCLUDED          │
├──────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
│ 7. CMMC L2 COMPLIANCE            │ ✓ FULL SUPPORT      │ ✓ FULL SUPPORT      │ ✓ FULL SUPPORT      │
├──────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
│ 8. COMPLIANCE AUTOMATION         │ ⚠ BASIC             │ ✓ ADVANCED          │ ⚠ MANAGED SERVICE   │
│                                  │   Manual processes  │   Comply AI, 200+   │   CloudFit handles  │
│                                  │                     │   auto tests        │                     │
├──────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
│ 9. 24/7 MONITORING               │ ⚠ BUSINESS HOURS    │ ✓ 24/5 SUPPORT      │ ✓ 24/7 MONITORING   │
├──────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤

├──────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
