│                                  │   Install what you  │   Your choice       │   ONLY (Standard    │
│                                  │   need              │                     │   Worker Persona)   │
├──────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
│ 13. SCALING USERS                │ $1,614/user added   │ $0 (up to 300 FTE)  │ $3,000/user added   │
├──────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
│ 14. CHANGE MANAGEMENT            │ ✓ YOUR PROCESS      │ ✓ YOUR PROCESS      │ ✗ CLOUDFIT APPROVAL │
│     (Add new software)           │   MSP support       │   Platform tracks   │   Required (slow)   │
│                                  │   available         │                     │                     │
├──────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
│ 15. VENDOR LOCK-IN RISK          │ ⚠ MODERATE          │ ✓ LOW               │ ✗ HIGH              │
│                                  │   Microsoft Partner │   Platform agnostic │   No admin access   │
│                                  │   of Record         │   Easy to switch    │   Total dependence  │
└──────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────┴─────────────────────┴─────────────────────┘

BOTTOM LINE:
├──────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────┬─────────────────────┬─────────────────────┤
│ BEST FOR:                        │ BALANCED SOLUTION   │ MAXIMUM CONTROL     │ LOWEST COST         │
│                                  │ • Reasonable cost   │ • Best technology   │ • Fully managed     │
│                                  │ • Software freedom  │ • You have IT staff │ • Simple needs only │
│                                  │ • MSP support       │ • Max flexibility   │ • No admin burden   │
├──────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
│ NOT RECOMMENDED IF:              │ • Need 1-year term  │ • No IT staff       │ • Need custom       │
│                                  │ • Avoid 3-year lock │ • Want turnkey      │   software          │
│                                  │                     │ • Budget limited    │ • Want admin access │
└──────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────┴─────────────────────┴─────────────────────┘

CRITICAL DECISION FACTORS
1. SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS ARE THE KEY DIFFERENTIATOR
Question for Safari Circuits Engineering Team:
Can your team operate with ONLY Microsoft 365 apps, Teams, and Edge browser? Or do you need:

✅ Gerber Viewer (for PCB design files)
✅ AutoCAD or other CAD software
✅ ERP system integration
✅ Engineering calculators or simulation tools
✅ Version control clients (Git, SVN)
✅ Specialized manufacturing software

If you need ANY of the above:

❌ DO NOT choose CloudFit - You'll face constant approval delays, extra costs, and friction
✅ Choose Ardalyst or Secureframe - Install what you need with proper change management

If you ONLY need M365/Teams/Edge:

✅ CloudFit saves $10K-130K and removes all admin burden


2. ADMINISTRATIVE CONTROL vs. COST TRADE-OFF
VendorControl LevelCostTrade-offArdalyst⚠️ Moderate (you have admin access, MSP manages infrastructure)💰 $191KBalanced: reasonable control at reasonable costSecureframe✅ Full (your infrastructure, your rules)💰💰💰 $310K+Maximum control, highest costCloudFit❌ None (CloudFit exclusive admin rights)💰 $180KCheapest, but zero control
The Fundamental Question:
Is saving $10K-20K over 3 years worth giving up ALL administrative control?
What "No Admin Control" Means:

You cannot troubleshoot your own systems
You cannot install software on your timeline
You cannot configure settings
You depend entirely on CloudFit for EVERYTHING
If CloudFit says no, you're stuck


3. NIST 800-171 SOFTWARE INSTALLATION REQUIREMENTS
If Safari Circuits has admin rights (Ardalyst/Secureframe), you must implement:

Change Management Process (CM.L2-3.4.3)

Written change request for each software installation
Security review and approval workflow
Documentation of installations
Rollback plans


Configuration Management (CM.L2-3.4.1, CM.L2-3.4.2)

Maintain approved software list (CMDB)
Version control
Track deviations


Least Functionality (CM.L2-3.4.6)

Justify necessity of each software
Install only required components
Disable unnecessary features


Application Whitelisting (CM.L2-3.4.8)

Configure Windows Defender Application Control or AppLocker
Add approved software to whitelist
Block unauthorized executables


Software Integrity Verification (SI.L2-3.13.3)

Verify download integrity (SHA-256 hash)
Check digital signatures
File integrity monitoring


Vulnerability Management (RA.L2-3.11.2)

Scan for CVEs after installation
Patch management process
Vendor security monitoring


Audit and Accountability (AU.L2-3.3.1)

Log installation events
Track admin actions
Retain logs for 1+ year


Access Control (AC.L2-3.1.1, AC.L2-3.1.2)

Principle of least privilege
Role-based access
User cannot install without admin rights



Sample Change Request Template:
Software Installation Request
─────────────────────────────
Software Name: Gerber Viewer 4
Vendor: [Vendor name]
Version: 4.2.1
Purpose: View PCB design files for manufacturing review
Requested by: [Engineer name]
Business justification: Required for engineering review of CUI-marked circuit designs
Systems affected: Engineering workstations (AVD session hosts)

Security Review:
- Downloaded from: [Official vendor URL]
- SHA-256 hash verified: [Yes/No]
- Digital signature valid: [Yes/No]
- Antivirus scan: [Clean/Issues]
- Known vulnerabilities: [None found]
- Network requirements: [None/Offline only]

Approval:
- Security Officer: [Name, Date]
- IT Manager: [Name, Date]

Installation:
- Installed by: [Admin name]
- Installation date: [Date]
- Systems: [List of device IDs or AVD hosts]
- AppLocker rule added: [Yes]
- CMDB updated: [Yes]
With CloudFit: You don't manage this - CloudFit does. Pro: No burden. Con: No control or visibility.
With Ardalyst: You can implement this process with MSP support.
With Secureframe: You implement this, platform tracks compliance.

RECOMMENDATION FRAMEWORK
SCENARIO 1: Engineering Team Needs Specialized Software
Recommended: ARDALYST
Reasoning:

✅ Full Windows environment - install Gerber Viewer, CAD, ERP, etc.
✅ You have admin rights with proper change management
✅ MSP support to help implement NIST 800-171 controls
✅ IT help desk included
✅ All infrastructure included (M365, AVD, endpoint management)
✅ Reasonable cost ($191K vs $310K for Secureframe)
⚠️ 3-year commitment required

Why not Secureframe?

60% more expensive when infrastructure costs added
Requires IT staff to manage infrastructure
Safari Circuits appears to be SMB without large IT department

Why not CloudFit?

"Standard Worker Persona" too restrictive
Software approval process will create constant friction
Unknown additional costs for custom software


SCENARIO 2: Engineering Team Only Needs M365/Teams/Web Apps
Recommended: CLOUDFIT
Reasoning:

✅ Saves $10K-130K over alternatives
✅ Zero admin burden - fully managed
✅ Fast deployment (30 days)
✅ 24/7 monitoring included
✅ 1-year renewable contract (flexibility)
⚠️ You give up all admin control
⚠️ Total vendor dependence

Why not Ardalyst?

$10K more expensive for features you don't need
3-year lock-in unnecessary if needs are simple

Why not Secureframe?

$130K more expensive
Overkill for simple needs
Requires IT staff to manage


SCENARIO 3: Safari Circuits Has In-House IT Team & Wants Maximum Control
Recommended: SECUREFRAME
Reasoning:

✅ Best-in-class compliance automation (Comply AI, 200+ tests)
✅ Maximum flexibility and control
✅ Platform-agnostic (multi-cloud, any tooling)
✅ Low vendor lock-in
✅ Advanced risk/vendor/policy management
✅ 1-year contract option
⚠️ Most expensive when infrastructure added
⚠️ Requires IT staff to deploy and manage infrastructure

Why not Ardalyst?

Less sophisticated technology
Microsoft-centric (less flexible)
3-year mandatory commitment

Why not CloudFit?

No control
Limited to CloudFit's standard configuration
High vendor lock-in


CRITICAL QUESTIONS TO ASK VENDORS
For CloudFit (Most Important):

"We need to install Gerber Viewer 4 for PCB design review. What is the complete process, timeline, and cost to add this software?"
"Can you provide a list of all non-standard software you've approved for other manufacturing/engineering clients?"
"What software requests have you denied, and what were the reasons?"
"If we have a production emergency requiring immediate software installation, what is your SLA?"
"Can we get read-only admin access for troubleshooting, or is ALL access exclusively CloudFit?"
"What happens if we outgrow easyCMMC and want to transition to self-managed? Can we keep our GCC-High environment?"
"Provide 3 customer references from manufacturing or DoD contractors with similar software needs."

For Ardalyst:

"What is your change management process for installing software like Gerber Viewer?"
"Do we get direct admin rights, or does everything go through your support team?"
"Is there a per-software-installation fee, or is it covered under the service?"
"What are the early termination terms if we need to exit before 3 years?"
"Can you provide customer references from electronics/PCB manufacturers?"
"What is your team's hands-on experience with C3PAO assessments?"
"What happens to our Microsoft 365 licenses if we don't renew after 3 years?"

For Secureframe:

"What is the TRUE total cost including Microsoft 365 E5 GCC-High, Azure Virtual Desktop, endpoint management, and SIEM?"
"Do you provide OR manage virtual desktop infrastructure, or just monitor compliance?"
"Can you recommend Azure/M365 partners to help us deploy the required infrastructure?"
"What is the typical implementation timeline when infrastructure must be built from scratch?"
"How many customers do you have successfully operating in GCC-High environments?"
"Can you provide customer references from manufacturing companies with 10-50 employees?"


NEXT STEPS FOR SAFARI CIRCUITS
1. Internal Assessment (CRITICAL - DO THIS FIRST)
Convene meeting with:

Engineering team
IT/Systems staff
Finance
Operations

Answer these questions:

What software does engineering ACTUALLY need beyond M365/Teams/Edge?

List every application currently used
List applications needed in next 12 months
Identify which handle CUI


Do we have IT staff capable of managing infrastructure?

If yes: Secureframe viable
If no: Ardalyst or CloudFit


How often do we need to install new software?

Weekly/Monthly: Need admin flexibility
Rarely: Managed service acceptable


What is our tolerance for vendor lock-in?

Low tolerance: Avoid CloudFit
Acceptable: CloudFit viable


What is our budget ceiling?

$180K: CloudFit only option
$190K: Ardalyst accessible
$310K+: All options open



2. Vendor Follow-Up
Send questions listed above to each vendor, request:

Written responses
3 customer references (manufacturing/DoD contractors)
Demo/walkthrough session
Detailed SOW with all assumptions

3. Reference Calls
For each vendor, speak with 2-3 customers and ask:

"What software have you successfully installed beyond the standard package?"
"How long does software approval/installation typically take?"
"Have you ever had a software request denied? Why?"
"What has been your biggest challenge working with this vendor?"
"Would you choose them again knowing what you know now?"

4. Decision Timeline

Week 1: Internal software requirements assessment
Week 2: Send questions to all vendors
Week 3: Vendor responses + reference calls
Week 4: Final decision + contract negotiation

5. Decision Criteria Weighting (Suggested)

Software flexibility: 30%
Total cost: 25%
Administrative control: 20%
Compliance capabilities: 15%
Vendor maturity/support: 10%


KEY INSIGHTS FROM ANALYSIS
Insight 1: CloudFit's Price is Misleading
CloudFit appears cheapest ($180K) but this assumes:

You only need M365/Teams/Edge
You're comfortable with zero admin access
You won't need custom software
You're okay with total vendor dependence

Reality: For a manufacturing/engineering company, the hidden costs of software approval delays, extra fees, and operational friction likely exceed the $10K savings.
Insight 2: Secureframe's Quote is Incomplete
Secureframe quoted $210K but this is software only. True cost:

Platform: $210,000
M365 E5 GCC-High: +$67,000
Azure Virtual Desktop: +$15,000-30,000
Endpoint management: +$3,000
IT support/MSP: +$45,000-60,000
TRUE TOTAL: ~$340,000-370,000

Secureframe makes sense ONLY if you:

Already have M365 E5 and infrastructure
Have IT staff to manage it
Want best-in-class technology

Insight 3: Ardalyst is the "Goldilocks" Option
Not too expensive, not too restrictive:

Complete infrastructure included
Reasonable admin control
Full Windows environment
MSP support for compliance
All-in price of $191K (vs $180K CloudFit, $310K+ Secureframe)

The $10K premium over CloudFit buys:

Software installation freedom
Admin rights
Full Windows environment
No approval delays

For an engineering company, this is worth it.

INSTRUCTIONS FOR CLAUDE CODE (PRESENTATION BUILDER)
When the user asks you to help build a presentation using this knowledge base:
Available Presentation Templates:

Executive Summary (5-7 slides) - For C-suite decision makers
Detailed Analysis (15-20 slides) - For technical/procurement teams
Board Presentation (10-12 slides) - For board approval
Vendor Comparison Only (8-10 slides) - Focus on side-by-side comparison

Slide Structure Recommendations:
Executive Summary Template:

Title slide
Problem statement (CMMC L2 requirement)
Vendors evaluated (3 options)
Comparison matrix (15-point table)
Cost comparison (3-year TCO)
Recommendation with reasoning
Next steps

Detailed Analysis Template:

Title slide
Background (CMMC L2, CUI, Safari Circuits context)
Evaluation criteria
Vendor 1: Ardalyst overview
Vendor 1: Pricing breakdown
Vendor 1: Pros/cons
Vendor 2: Secureframe overview
Vendor 2: Pricing breakdown (including hidden infrastructure costs)
Vendor 2: Pros/cons
Vendor 3: CloudFit overview
Vendor 3: Pricing breakdown
Vendor 3: Pros/cons
Side-by-side comparison matrix
Software flexibility analysis (Gerber Viewer use case)
NIST 800-171 compliance requirements
Risk assessment
Recommendation
Implementation timeline
Critical questions for vendors
Next steps

Design Guidelines:

Use clean, professional layouts
Color coding: Green = advantage, Yellow = adequate, Red = concern
Include the 15-point comparison table formatted for readability
Use icons for key concepts (cost, control, compliance, support)
Keep text minimal - bullet points only
Use charts for cost comparisons (bar charts work well)

Data to Include:

All pricing data from the comparison table
The 15-point evaluation matrix
Software flexibility comparison (critical for Safari Circuits)
NIST 800-171 requirements summary
Vendor contact information

Key Messages to Emphasize:

For Engineering Company: Software flexibility is critical
Cost Isn't Everything: CloudFit is cheapest but most restrictive
Hidden Costs: Secureframe requires separate infrastructure purchases
Balanced Choice: Ardalyst offers best value for Safari Circuits' needs

Output Format:

PowerPoint (.pptx) preferred
Google Slides compatible
PDF export for distribution
Include speaker notes with talking points

When User Provides Template:

Parse their template structure
Map content from this knowledge base to their slides
Maintain their branding/colors
Preserve their formatting style
Fill in data points and comparison tables
Generate speaker notes if requested


DOCUMENT VERSION CONTROL

Last Updated: May 11, 2026

END OF KNOWLEDGE BASE

HOW TO USE THIS PROMPT:
Save this entire prompt as your project knowledge base. When working with Claude Code or any AI assistant:

Provide this prompt as context
Ask for specific outputs:

"Create an executive presentation comparing the 3 vendors"

input.content:
# Safari Circuits - CMMC Level 2 Compliance Vendor Evaluation

## Project Overview

**Goal:** Evaluate and select CMMC Level 2 compliance vendor for Safari Circuits
**Timeline:** May 2026 - Q3 2026 (Decision: Week 4, Implementation: 30-90 days)
**Stakeholders:**
- Eduardo Marquez (IT Systems Engineering Manager) - Project Lead
- Scott (IT Director) - Approval Authority
- Engineering Team - Requirements Input
- External C3PAO Auditor - Assessment Partner

**Status:** Active - Vendor Evaluation Phase

---

## Context for Claude

Safari Circuits, LLC is a manufacturing/electronics company in Otsego, Michigan that:
- Manufactures circuit boards and handles CUI (Controlled Unclassified Information)
- Requires CMMC Level 2 certification for Department of Defense contracts
- Must comply with NIST 800-171 (110 security controls)
- Has 20 CUI users requiring secure access
- Needs specialized engineering software (Gerber Viewer for PCB design, potentially CAD tools)

### Critical Technical Requirements
- Microsoft 365 GCC-High environment
- Azure Virtual Desktop infrastructure
- Ability to install specialized engineering software
- Ongoing compliance management and C3PAO assessment support

---

## Workspace Structure

- **00-Inbox**: Quick captures, meeting notes, vendor emails, questions to process

# Safari Circuits - CMMC Level 2 Compliance Vendor Evaluation

## Project Overview

**Goal:** Evaluate and select CMMC Level 2 compliance vendor for Safari Circuits
**Timeline:** May 2026 - Q3 2026 (Decision: Week 4, Implementation: 30-90 days)
**Stakeholders:**
- Eduardo Marquez (IT Systems Engineering Manager) - Project Lead
- Scott (IT Director) - Approval Authority
- Engineering Team - Requirements Input
- External C3PAO Auditor - Assessment Partner

**Status:** Active - Vendor Evaluation Phase

---

## Context for Claude

Safari Circuits, LLC is a manufacturing/electronics company in Otsego, Michigan that:
- Manufactures circuit boards and handles CUI (Controlled Unclassified Information)
- Requires CMMC Level 2 certification for Department of Defense contracts
- Must comply with NIST 800-171 (110 security controls)
- Has 20 CUI users requiring secure access
- Needs specialized engineering software (Gerber Viewer for PCB design, potentially CAD tools)

### Critical Technical Requirements
- Microsoft 365 GCC-High environment
- Azure Virtual Desktop infrastructure
- Ability to install specialized engineering software
- Ongoing compliance management and C3PAO assessment support

---

## Workspace Structure

- **00-Inbox**: Quick captures, meeting notes, vendor emails, questions to process
- **01-Research**: Vendor proposals, analysis documents, reference materials
- **02-Drafts**: Work-in-progress presentations, comparison reports, decision memos
- **03-Deliverables**: Finalized outputs ready for team SharePoint (executive summaries, board presentations)

---

## Team SharePoint Locations

3. **Keep it empty-ish** - Inbox should be a temporary holding area, not permanent storage

## Example Files

- `vendor-questions-[date].md` - Questions to ask vendors
- `meeting-notes-[date].md` - Notes from stakeholder meetings
- `ideas-[date].md` - Random ideas or considerations
- `engineering-feedback-[date].md` - Input from engineering team

# Research - Analysis & Source Documents

This folder contains:

- Vendor proposals and quotes
- Comparison analyses
- Cost breakdowns
- Customer references and feedback
- NIST 800-171 documentation
- CMMC Level 2 requirements
- Technical specifications
- Competitive analysis

## Recommended Structure

```
01-Research/
├── vendor-proposals/
│   ├── Ardalyst-Tesseract-Quote.pdf
│   ├── Secureframe-Pricing.pdf
│   └── CloudFit-SOW-and-Quote.pdf
├── cost-analysis-[date].xlsx
├── vendor-comparison-detailed-[date].md
├── customer-references/
│   ├── Ardalyst-references.md
│   ├── Secureframe-references.md
│   └── CloudFit-references.md
├── requirements/
│   ├── NIST-800-171-checklist.xlsx
│   ├── CMMC-L2-domains.md
│   └── engineering-software-requirements.md
└── technical-specs/
    ├── Azure-AVD-requirements.md
    ├── M365-GCC-High-specs.md
    └── network-architecture.pdf
```

## Working with Source Documents

When adding vendor proposals or source documents:

1. **Use descriptive filenames:** `Vendor-DocumentType-Date.pdf`
2. **Create summary notes:** For each PDF, create a `.md` file with key points
3. **Tag with metadata:** Date received, contact person, expiration date
4. **Link in CLAUDE.md:** Add important docs to "Source Documents" section

## Analysis Documents

Create comparison analyses here:
- Cost breakdowns with TCO calculations
- Feature comparisons (side-by-side tables)
- Risk assessments
- Technical requirement mappings
- Decision matrices

# Drafts - Work in Progress

This folder contains documents being actively developed:

- Presentations (PowerPoint, Google Slides)
- Reports and memos
- Decision documents
- Comparison matrices
- Executive summaries
- Board presentations

## ⚠️ IMPORTANT: Privacy Rule

**Everything in this folder is PRIVATE and NOT for team SharePoint.**

- Feel free to include rough thinking, comments, questions
- Iterate with Claude Code without worrying about others seeing it
- Only move to `03-Deliverables/` when ready for sharing

## Naming Convention

Use descriptive names with dates:
- `executive-summary-draft-2026-05-15.pptx`
- `vendor-comparison-v2-2026-05-18.pptx`
- `board-presentation-draft-2026-05-20.pptx`

to emphasize software flexibility concerns...

## When to Move to Deliverables

- [ ] Ready for team SharePoint publication

# Deliverables - Ready for Publication

This folder contains **finalized documents ready for team SharePoint**.

## Publication Process

1. **Finalize in 02-Drafts/**
2. **Move to this folder** when ready
3. **Verify** document is clean and professional
4. **Copy to team SharePoint** using commands in `_links.md`
5. **Notify stakeholders**

## Checklist Before Publishing

  - `.pdf` for read-only documents

## Current Deliverables

## Publishing Command

```powershell
# From project directory
cd "C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\00_Claude\05-Projects\Safari-CMMC-Level-2"

Copy-Item .\03-Deliverables\*.* `
          'S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Projects\CMMC-Level-2\' -Force
```

## After Publishing

- [ ] Email stakeholders with location
- [ ] Update project CLAUDE.md decision log if applicable

input.content:
# Safari Circuits Project Workflow

How to create and manage Safari Circuits company projects using the personal vault + team SharePoint approach.

---

## Overview

**Privacy Model:**
- Work privately in personal Obsidian vault (`00_Claude\05-Projects`)
- Only publish finalized deliverables to team SharePoint
- Project memory syncs via Git (personal-claude-docs), not SharePoint

**Benefits:**
- Team doesn't see exploratory questions, rough drafts, or thinking process
- Can't accidentally share half-baked ideas
- Project memory follows you home ↔ work via Git
- Obsidian integration for linking, templates, graph view
- Clear publishing flow with explicit approval

---

## Creating a New Safari Project

### Step 1: Create Project in Personal Vault

```powershell
# Set variables
$projectName = "Safari-{Project-Name}"  # e.g., "Safari-CMMC-Level-2"
$vaultPath = "C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\00_Claude"
$projectPath = "$vaultPath\05-Projects\$projectName"

# Create project structure
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path "$projectPath\00-Inbox" -Force
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path "$projectPath\01-Research" -Force
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path "$projectPath\02-Drafts" -Force
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path "$projectPath\03-Deliverables" -Force

Write-Host "✓ Project created: $projectPath" -ForegroundColor Green
```

### Step 2: Create Project Files from Template

Use the template at: `personal-claude-docs/.claude/enterprise/reference/safari-project-template.md`

Copy and customize:

```powershell
# Copy template
$templatePath = "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\reference\safari-project-template.md"
$claudeMdPath = "$projectPath\CLAUDE.md"

Copy-Item $templatePath $claudeMdPath

# Edit with VS Code or Obsidian
code $claudeMdPath
# OR
obsidian://open?vault=00_Claude&file=05-Projects/$projectName/CLAUDE.md
```

Customize these sections:
- Project Overview (goal, timeline, stakeholders, status)
- Context for Claude (background, terminology, requirements)
- Team SharePoint Locations (where deliverables will be published)
- Common Tasks & Prompts (frequent operations)
- Decision Log (key decisions as project progresses)

### Step 3: Create SharePoint Links File

```powershell
# Create _links.md with SharePoint references
$linksTemplate = @"
# Team SharePoint Links & Publishing

## Published Project Folder (Team SharePoint)

**Primary Location:**
``````
S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Projects\{folder-name}
``````

> ⚠️ **IMPORTANT:** This is a TEAM SharePoint. Only publish finalized deliverables here.

## Publishing Workflow

[See Safari-CMMC-Level-2\_links.md for complete publishing instructions]

Quick publish command:
``````powershell
Copy-Item .\03-Deliverables\*.* 'S:\...\Projects\{folder-name}\' -Force
``````
"@

$linksTemplate | Out-File "$projectPath\_links.md" -Encoding utf8
```

### Step 4: Add PowerShell Alias

Edit your PowerShell profile (`$PROFILE`):

```powershell
function safari-{shortname} {
    cd "C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\00_Claude\05-Projects\Safari-{Project-Name}"
    claude
}
```

Example:
```powershell
function cmmc {
    cd "C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\00_Claude\05-Projects\Safari-CMMC-Level-2"
    claude
}
```

Then reload: `. $PROFILE`

Usage: Just type the alias to instantly open that project in Claude Code.

---

## Daily Workflow

### Working on the Project

```powershell
# Navigate to project (or use alias)
cd "C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\00_Claude\05-Projects\Safari-{Project-Name}"

# Start Claude Code
claude
```

**What happens:**
- Claude loads project-specific CLAUDE.md
- Memory accumulates at `~/.claude/projects/C--Users-...-{Project-Name}/`
- This memory syncs via personal-claude-docs Git (not SharePoint!)

### Capturing Ideas and Notes

```
User: "Note to self: Engineering team confirmed they need Gerber Viewer"
Claude: [Captures to 00-Inbox/]

User: "/learn We decided to prioritize MFA because it affects 15 CMMC controls"
Claude: [Captures to project memory]
```

### Creating Deliverables

```
User: "Create an executive presentation comparing the 3 vendors. Save to 02-Drafts/"
Claude: [Creates presentation-draft.pptx in 02-Drafts/]

User: "Update the presentation to emphasize software flexibility. Review and polish for board presentation."
Claude: [Iterates on draft]

User: "Looks good. Move to 03-Deliverables/ and prepare for publication."
Claude: [Moves file, updates filename to remove "draft"]
```

### Publishing to Team SharePoint

```powershell
# 1. Review deliverable one last time
Start-Process .\03-Deliverables\executive-summary.pptx

# 2. Create team folder (first time only)
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path 'S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Projects\{folder-name}' -Force

# 3. Copy to SharePoint
Copy-Item .\03-Deliverables\executive-summary.pptx `
          'S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Projects\{folder-name}\' -Force

# 4. Verify
explorer 'S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Projects\{folder-name}'

# 5. Email stakeholders
# Subject: {Project Name} - {Document Name} Available for Review
# Body: The {document name} is now available at S:\...\Projects\{folder-name}\{filename}
```

---

## Project Memory Sync

### How It Works

```
Personal Vault Project:
C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\00_Claude\05-Projects\Safari-CMMC-Level-2\
  ↓
Claude Code working directory
  ↓
Project memory stored at:
~/.claude/projects/C--Users-emarquez-OneDrive-...-00_Claude-05-Projects-Safari-CMMC-Level-2/
  ↓ symlink
personal-claude-docs/.claude/project-memory/C--Users-emarquez-OneDrive-...-00_Claude-05-Projects-Safari-CMMC-Level-2/
  ↓ Git sync
GitHub (personal-claude-docs repo)
  ↓ Git pull on other computer
Home computer gets same project memory!
```

### Syncing Across Computers

**On work computer (after working on project):**
```powershell
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git add .claude/project-memory/
git commit -m "Update: CMMC project progress"
git push
```

**On home computer:**
```powershell
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git pull

# Project memory is now synced!
cd "D:\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\01_Claude_Home\05-Projects\Safari-CMMC-Level-2"
claude
# Claude remembers all decisions and context from work computer
```

---

## Privacy Guidelines

### ✅ Safe to Publish (Team SharePoint)

- Final presentations
- Executive summaries
- Decision memos (approved)
- Cost comparisons (approved for sharing)
- Vendor comparison matrices
- Implementation timelines
- Board-ready documents

### ❌ Keep Private (Personal Vault Only)

- Rough drafts with comments
- Exploratory questions to Claude ("What if we chose CloudFit instead?")
- "What if" scenarios
- Budget sensitivity analysis
- Internal strategy discussions
- Vendor negotiation notes
- Personal opinions/concerns
- Work-in-progress documents
- Any document with "DRAFT" watermarks

### When in Doubt

**Ask:** "Would I be comfortable with the entire company seeing this?"
- **Yes:** Safe to publish to SharePoint
- **No:** Keep in personal vault

---

## Recommended Folder Structure

```
00_Claude/
└── 05-Projects/
    ├── Safari-CMMC-Level-2/
    │   ├── CLAUDE.md                     ← Project context + knowledge base
    │   ├── _links.md                     ← SharePoint locations + publish commands
    │   ├── 00-Inbox/                     ← Quick captures, meeting notes
    │   │   ├── README.md
    │   │   ├── vendor-questions-2026-05-15.md
    │   │   └── meeting-notes-2026-05-18.md
    │   ├── 01-Research/                  ← Source docs, analysis
    │   │   ├── README.md
    │   │   ├── vendor-proposals/
    │   │   ├── cost-analysis.xlsx
    │   │   └── requirements/
    │   ├── 02-Drafts/                    ← WIP documents (PRIVATE)
    │   │   ├── README.md
    │   │   ├── executive-summary-draft.pptx
    │   │   └── board-presentation-v2.pptx
    │   └── 03-Deliverables/              ← Ready to publish
    │       ├── README.md
    │       └── executive-summary.pptx
    ├── Safari-Azure-Migration/
    │   └── [same structure]
    └── Safari-BeFirst-Modernization/
        └── [same structure]
```

---

## Common Prompts

### Start New Project
```
I'm starting a new Safari project: [Project Name]. Help me create the directory structure
and customize the CLAUDE.md template with the following details: [context, goals, stakeholders].
```

### Generate Deliverable
```
Create a [presentation/report/memo] for [audience] covering [topic].
Include: [key points].
Save to 02-Drafts/[filename]-[date].
```

### Prepare for Publishing
```
Review the [document name] in 02-Drafts/. Remove any internal notes or rough thinking.
Polish for [audience] and move to 03-Deliverables/ when ready.
```

### Sync Project Memory
```
I'm switching to my home computer. Commit the project memory changes with message
"Update: [project name] progress - [what was accomplished]"
```

---

## Troubleshooting

### Project Memory Not Syncing

**Symptom:** Claude doesn't remember decisions from other computer

**Fix:**
```powershell
# 1. Verify symlink exists
Get-Item ~/.claude/projects
# Should show: Mode: l----- Target: personal-claude-docs/.claude/project-memory

# 2. Check git status
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git status
# Should show changes in .claude/project-memory/

# 3. Manually sync
git add .claude/project-memory/
git commit -m "Sync project memory"
git push
```

### Can't Access Team SharePoint from PowerShell

**Symptom:** `Copy-Item` fails with access denied

**Fix:**
1. Verify S: drive is mapped correctly
2. Check SharePoint permissions
3. Try opening in Explorer first: `explorer 'S:\...'`
4. Manually copy files if needed

### Vault Not Found

**Symptom:** `cd` command fails to find vault path

**Fix:**
```powershell
# Verify vault exists
Test-Path "C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\00_Claude"

# If not synced yet, wait for OneDrive sync
# Or check if path is different on this computer
```

---

## Quick Reference Card

| Task | Command |
|------|---------|
| **Start project** | `{alias}` (e.g., `cmmc`) |
| **Quick capture** | Add file to `00-Inbox/` |
| **Work on deliverable** | Create in `02-Drafts/` |
| **Publish to SharePoint** | Move to `03-Deliverables/`, then `Copy-Item` |
| **Sync memory** | `cd ~/personal-claude-docs && git add .claude/project-memory/ && git commit && git push` |
| **Open SharePoint** | `explorer 'S:\...\Projects\{folder}'` |

---

*This workflow keeps your thinking private while collaborating effectively with the team.*

## Daily Workflow

### Working on the Project

```powershell
# Navigate to project (or use alias)
cd "C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\00_Claude\05-Projects\Safari-{Project-Name}"

```

**What happens:**
- Claude loads project-specific CLAUDE.md
- Memory accumulates at `~/.claude/projects/C--Users-...-{Project-Name}/`
- This memory syncs via personal-claude-docs Git (not SharePoint!)

```

### Publishing to Team SharePoint

## Privacy Guidelines

### ✅ Safe to Publish (Team SharePoint)

- Final presentations
- Executive summaries
- Decision memos (approved)
- Cost comparisons (approved for sharing)
- Vendor comparison matrices
- Implementation timelines
- Board-ready documents

### ❌ Keep Private (Personal Vault Only)

- Rough drafts with comments
- Exploratory questions to Claude ("What if we chose CloudFit instead?")
- "What if" scenarios
- Budget sensitivity analysis
- Internal strategy discussions
- Vendor negotiation notes
- Personal opinions/concerns
- Work-in-progress documents
- Any document with "DRAFT" watermarks

### When in Doubt

**Ask:** "Would I be comfortable with the entire company seeing this?"
- **Yes:** Safe to publish to SharePoint
- **No:** Keep in personal vault

00_Claude/
└── 05-Projects/
    ├── Safari-CMMC-Level-2/
    │   ├── CLAUDE.md                     ← Project context + knowledge base
    │   ├── _links.md                     ← SharePoint locations + publish commands
    │   ├── 00-Inbox/                     ← Quick captures, meeting notes
    │   │   ├── README.md
    │   │   ├── vendor-questions-2026-05-15.md
    │   │   └── meeting-notes-2026-05-18.md
    │   ├── 01-Research/                  ← Source docs, analysis
    │   │   ├── README.md
    │   │   ├── vendor-proposals/
    │   │   ├── cost-analysis.xlsx
    │   │   └── requirements/
    │   ├── 02-Drafts/                    ← WIP documents (PRIVATE)
    │   │   ├── README.md
    │   │   ├── executive-summary-draft.pptx
    │   │   └── board-presentation-v2.pptx
    │   └── 03-Deliverables/              ← Ready to publish
    │       ├── README.md
    │       └── executive-summary.pptx
    ├── Safari-Azure-Migration/
    │   └── [same structure]
    └── Safari-BeFirst-Modernization/
        └── [same structure]
```

## Common Prompts

### Start New Project
```
I'm starting a new Safari project: [Project Name]. Help me create the directory structure
and customize the CLAUDE.md template with the following details: [context, goals, stakeholders].
```

### Generate Deliverable
```
Create a [presentation/report/memo] for [audience] covering [topic].
Include: [key points].
Save to 02-Drafts/[filename]-[date].
```

### Prepare for Publishing
```
Review the [document name] in 02-Drafts/. Remove any internal notes or rough thinking.
Polish for [audience] and move to 03-Deliverables/ when ready.
```

### Sync Project Memory
```
I'm switching to my home computer. Commit the project memory changes with message
"Update: [project name] progress - [what was accomplished]"
```

---

## Troubleshooting

### Project Memory Not Syncing

**Symptom:** Claude doesn't remember decisions from other computer

**Fix:**
```powershell
# 1. Verify symlink exists
Get-Item ~/.claude/projects
# Should show: Mode: l----- Target: personal-claude-docs/.claude/project-memory

# 2. Check git status
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git status
# Should show changes in .claude/project-memory/

# 3. Manually sync
git add .claude/project-memory/
git commit -m "Sync project memory"
git push
```

### Can't Access Team SharePoint from PowerShell

**Symptom:** `Copy-Item` fails with access denied

**Fix:**
1. Verify S: drive is mapped correctly
2. Check SharePoint permissions
3. Try opening in Explorer first: `explorer 'S:\...'`
4. Manually copy files if needed

### Vault Not Found

**Symptom:** `cd` command fails to find vault path

**Fix:**
```powershell
# Verify vault exists
Test-Path "C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\00_Claude"

# If not synced yet, wait for OneDrive sync
# Or check if path is different on this computer
```

---

## Quick Reference Card

| Task | Command |
|------|---------|
| **Start project** | `{alias}` (e.g., `cmmc`) |
| **Quick capture** | Add file to `00-Inbox/` |
| **Work on deliverable** | Create in `02-Drafts/` |
| **Publish to SharePoint** | Move to `03-Deliverables/`, then `Copy-Item` |
| **Sync memory** | `cd ~/personal-claude-docs && git add .claude/project-memory/ && git commit && git push` |
| **Open SharePoint** | `explorer 'S:\...\Projects\{folder}'` |

---

*This workflow keeps your thinking private while collaborating effectively with the team.*

Yes! The **symlinks from your Obsidian vault to `~/.claude/`** need to be updated. Here's what needs to happen:

## Symlinks That Need Updating

The setup-personal.ps1 script creates bidirectional symlinks:

1. **`personal-claude-docs` → `~/.claude/`** ✅ These are fine (no change needed)
2. **Vault → `~/.claude/`** ❌ **These are broken and need recreation**

The vault symlinks that are now pointing to the wrong location:
- `{vault}/04-Commands/_claude-commands` → `~/.claude/commands`
- `{vault}/03-Skills/_claude-skills` → `~/.claude/skills`
- `{vault}/02-Learnings/_claude-learning` → `~/.claude/learning`
- `{vault}/05-Reference/_claude-reference` → `~/.claude/reference`

Since you renamed `00_Claude` → `00_Claude_Safari`, these symlinks are pointing to a path that no longer exists.

## Solution: Re-run Setup Script

Run the setup script with `-Force` to recreate the symlinks:

```powershell
# Re-run setup to fix vault symlinks
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\scripts\setup-personal.ps1" -Force

input.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

---

## Repository Purpose

**personal-claude-docs** (aka ClaudeDocs) is a shared Claude Code knowledge base synchronized across multiple computers via Git. It contains:

- Enterprise configuration (workflow, principles, patterns)
- Custom commands (`/status`, `/plan`, `/implement`, etc.)
- Reusable skills and agents
- Learning pipeline (ideas → best practices → enforced standards)
- Personal preferences and tech stack

This repo is **NOT** a project to build software. It's **infrastructure** that wires Claude Code to work consistently across home, work, and laptop computers.

---

## Architecture Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ personal-claude-docs (this repo)                                │
│   └── .claude/enterprise/   ← Shared config (git-synced)       │
│   └── .claude/users/         ← Personal prefs (git-synced)     │
│   └── .claude/scripts/       ← Setup and verification tools    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              │
                              │ Symlinked by setup-personal.ps1
                              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ~/.claude/ (machine-specific repo: ClaudeHome or ClaudeWork)    │
│   commands/    ──symlink──► personal-claude-docs/enterprise/commands
│   skills/      ──symlink──► personal-claude-docs/enterprise/skills
│   learning/    ──symlink──► personal-claude-docs/enterprise/learning
│   agents/      ──symlink──► personal-claude-docs/enterprise/agents
│   reference/   ──symlink──► personal-claude-docs/enterprise/reference
│   enterprise/  ──symlink──► personal-claude-docs/enterprise/
│   personal/    ──symlink──► personal-claude-docs/users/emarquez-personal/
│   CLAUDE.md    ← Auto-generated loader (@enterprise + @personal)
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              │
                              │ Mirrored via symlinks (for Obsidian editing)
                              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\{vault}/   (01_Claude_Home or 00_Claude_Safari) │
│   04-Commands/_claude-commands   ──symlink──► ~/.claude/commands
│   03-Skills/_claude-skills       ──symlink──► ~/.claude/skills
│   02-Learnings/_claude-learning  ──symlink──► ~/.claude/learning
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Three-layer sync:**
1. **Git** (primary): personal-claude-docs → GitHub → other computers
2. **Symlinks** (runtime): personal-claude-docs → ~/.claude/ (Claude Code reads here)
3. **Obsidian** (editing): ~/.claude/ → vault (optional, for visual editing)

**Key invariant:** Git is the source of truth. OneDrive mirrors are read-only for Claude.

---

## Setup and Maintenance

### Initial Setup on New Machine

```powershell
# 1. Clone this repo
git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeDocs.git "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs"

# 2. Run setup (auto-detects home vs work based on username)
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\scripts\setup-personal.ps1"

# 3. Verify setup
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\scripts\verify-sync.ps1" -Detailed
```

**What setup-personal.ps1 does:**
- Detects computer profile (home: `eduar`, work: `emarquez`, laptop: auto)
- Creates symlinks from `~/.claude/` → `personal-claude-docs/.claude/enterprise/`
- Generates `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` with `@enterprise` and `@personal` imports
- Links project memory to enable git-based sync of session state
- (Work only) Wires Safari Circuits organizational standards via `@safari` layer
- Falls back to junctions if admin rights unavailable
- Adds PowerShell profile shortcut (`claude-session` / `cs`)

### Daily Sync Workflow

```powershell
# Morning: Pull latest changes from other computer
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git pull

# During work: Claude Code uses symlinked content automatically

# Evening: Commit and push changes
git add .claude/enterprise/ .claude/users/
git commit -m "Learn: [what you discovered]"
git push
```

**No restart needed after pull** — symlinks keep `~/.claude/` pointing to latest content.

### Verification

```powershell
# Quick check
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\scripts\verify-sync.ps1"

# Detailed report (command count, symlink targets, git status)
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\scripts\verify-sync.ps1" -Detailed

# Manual check
Get-Item ~/.claude/commands
# Should show: Mode: l----- (symlink) Target: ...\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\commands
```

**Success criteria:**
- All symlinks resolve
- Command count matches across computers
- Git status shows latest commit from other machine after pull

See `.claude/enterprise/scripts/SYNC-TEST-GUIDE.md` for comprehensive testing procedures.

---

## Adding and Modifying Content

### Adding a Custom Command

```powershell
# 1. Create file in enterprise/commands/
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
New-Item .claude\enterprise\commands\my-command.md

# 2. Edit with standard format:
#    - Description
#    - Parameters
#    - Implementation
#    - Examples

# 3. Commit and push
git add .claude/enterprise/commands/my-command.md
git commit -m "Add /my-command for [purpose]"
git push

# 4. Use immediately in new session
# (Restart Claude Code or `/clear` to reload commands)
/my-command
```

**Command format:** See existing commands in `.claude/enterprise/commands/` for templates.

### Adding a Skill

```powershell
# 1. Create skill directory
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
New-Item -ItemType Directory .claude\enterprise\skills\my-skill

# 2. Create SKILL.md with skill definition
# See .claude/enterprise/skills/example-skill/SKILL.md for template

# 3. Commit
git add .claude/enterprise/skills/my-skill/
git commit -m "Add my-skill for [purpose]"
git push
```

### Capturing Learnings

**Quick capture:**
```powershell
# Append to ideas backlog (unvalidated notes)
Add-Content .claude\enterprise\learning\ideas\backlog.md "`n- [$(Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-dd')] Learning: [insight]"
git add .claude/enterprise/learning/ideas/backlog.md
git commit -m "Learn: [insight]"
git push
```

**Promotion pipeline:**
1. **Idea** → `.claude/enterprise/learning/ideas/backlog.md`
2. **Validated** → `.claude/enterprise/learning/best-practices/{topic}.md`
3. **Standard** → `.claude/enterprise/CLAUDE.md` (enforced by Claude)

See `.claude/enterprise/learning/PIPELINE.md` for full promotion criteria.

### Modifying Configuration

**Enterprise config (shared across all computers):**
```powershell
# Edit the master config
notepad $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\CLAUDE.md

# Commit
git add .claude/enterprise/CLAUDE.md
git commit -m "Update enterprise config: [what changed]"
git push
```

**Personal config (user preferences, tech stack):**
```powershell
# Edit personal layer
notepad $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\users\emarquez-personal\CLAUDE.md

# Commit
git add .claude/users/emarquez-personal/
git commit -m "Update personal prefs: [what changed]"
git push
```

**Machine-specific config (local paths only):**
```powershell
# This is auto-generated by setup-personal.ps1
# To regenerate:
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\scripts\setup-personal.ps1" -Force
```

---

## Key Files and Directories

| Path | Purpose | Sync Method |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `.claude/enterprise/CLAUDE.md` | Core workflow, principles, patterns | Git |
| `.claude/enterprise/commands/` | Custom `/commands` | Git |
| `.claude/enterprise/skills/` | Reusable workflows | Git |
| `.claude/enterprise/learning/` | Knowledge capture pipeline | Git |
| `.claude/enterprise/agents/` | Subagent definitions | Git |
| `.claude/enterprise/scripts/` | Setup and verification tools | Git |
| `.claude/users/emarquez-personal/CLAUDE.md` | Personal preferences | Git |
| `.claude/scripts/setup-personal.ps1` | Bootstrap script (called by machine-specific setup) | Git |
| `.claude/project-memory/` | Session state and project memory | Git (NEW: syncs across machines) |

**Do NOT commit to this repo:**
- Workspace-specific files from other projects
- API keys, credentials, secrets
- Large binary files
- Temporary test files

---

## Troubleshooting

### Symlinks Broken

**Symptom:** Commands don't work, verification fails

**Fix:**
```powershell
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\scripts\setup-personal.ps1" -Force
```

### Git Conflicts on Pull

**Symptom:** Merge conflicts when pulling changes

**Fix:**
```powershell
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git status          # Review conflicts
git stash           # Stash local changes if needed
git pull            # Pull clean
git stash pop       # Re-apply local changes
# Resolve conflicts manually, then:
git add .
git commit -m "Merge: resolve conflicts"
git push
```

### Different File Counts Between Computers

**Symptom:** Home has 13 commands, work has 8

**Fix:**
```powershell
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git fetch --all
git status  # Check if behind origin
git pull
```

### Commands Not Available After Pull

**Symptom:** Files exist but commands don't work in Claude Code

**Fix:** Restart Claude Code session or use `/clear` to reload

---

## Architecture Decisions

### Why Symlinks Instead of Git Clones?

**Problem:** Multiple repos (ClaudeHome, ClaudeWork, ClaudeDocs) would drift out of sync.

**Solution:** One shared repo (ClaudeDocs) symlinked into each machine's `~/.claude/`.
- Single source of truth
- Instant sync (no re-clone needed)
- Works with Obsidian vaults simultaneously

### Why Three Layers (Git + Symlinks + OneDrive)?

**Git:** Source of truth, cross-machine sync, version control
**Symlinks:** Runtime wiring (Claude Code reads from `~/.claude/`)
**OneDrive:** Optional visual editing in Obsidian, automatic backup

Each layer has a specific purpose and doesn't duplicate the others.

### Why Separate Enterprise and Personal Layers?

**Enterprise:** Shared workflow practices (Boris Cherny methodology, RPI pattern)
**Personal:** Tech stack, communication style, current focus areas
**Work:** Safari Circuits organizational standards (work computer only)

Separation allows:
- Reuse enterprise layer for other users/teams
- Customize personal prefs without affecting workflow
- Keep work context isolated to work machine

### Project Memory Sync (New)

Previously, `~/.claude/projects/` (project memory) was local-only.
Now it's symlinked to `.claude/project-memory/` and synced via Git.

**Benefit:** Session state and learnings follow you across machines.
**Trade-off:** Must manually resolve conflicts if working on same project from both computers simultaneously.

---

## Work Computer Integration (Safari Circuits)

On work computer (`emarquez` user), setup-personal.ps1 adds:

```powershell
~/.claude/safari/              → organizational-docs/.claude/
~/.claude/reference/safari/    → organizational-docs/shared/
~/.claude/CLAUDE.md includes   → @safari/CLAUDE.md
```

This wires organizational standards (branding, APIs, security policies) into Claude sessions.

**Requirement:** `organizational-docs` repo must exist at `~/organizational-docs`.

---

## References

- **Setup script:** `.claude/scripts/setup-personal.ps1`
- **Verification:** `.claude/enterprise/scripts/verify-sync.ps1`
- **Testing guide:** `.claude/enterprise/scripts/SYNC-TEST-GUIDE.md`
- **Learning pipeline:** `.claude/enterprise/learning/PIPELINE.md`
- **Enterprise config:** `.claude/enterprise/CLAUDE.md`
- **Personal config:** `.claude/users/emarquez-personal/CLAUDE.md`

**External sources:**
- [Boris Cherny's Claude Code workflow](https://x.com/bcherny/status/2007179832300581177)
- [Advanced Context Engineering](https://github.com/humanlayer/advanced-context-engineering-for-coding-agents)
- [12-Factor Agents](https://github.com/humanlayer/12-factor-agents)

See `.claude/enterprise/scripts/SYNC-TEST-GUIDE.md` for comprehensive testing procedures.

---

## Adding and Modifying Content

### Adding a Custom Command

```powershell
# 1. Create file in enterprise/commands/
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
New-Item .claude\enterprise\commands\my-command.md

# 2. Edit with standard format:
#    - Description
#    - Parameters
#    - Implementation
#    - Examples

# 3. Commit and push
git add .claude/enterprise/commands/my-command.md
git commit -m "Add /my-command for [purpose]"
git push

# 4. Use immediately in new session
# (Restart Claude Code or `/clear` to reload commands)
/my-command
```

**Command format:** See existing commands in `.claude/enterprise/commands/` for templates.

### Adding a Skill

```powershell
# 1. Create skill directory
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
New-Item -ItemType Directory .claude\enterprise\skills\my-skill

# 2. Create SKILL.md with skill definition
# See .claude/enterprise/skills/example-skill/SKILL.md for template

# 3. Commit
git add .claude/enterprise/skills/my-skill/
git commit -m "Add my-skill for [purpose]"
git push
```

### Capturing Learnings

**Quick capture:**
```powershell
# Append to ideas backlog (unvalidated notes)
Add-Content .claude\enterprise\learning\ideas\backlog.md "`n- [$(Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-dd')] Learning: [insight]"
git add .claude/enterprise/learning/ideas/backlog.md
git commit -m "Learn: [insight]"
git push
```

**Promotion pipeline:**
1. **Idea** → `.claude/enterprise/learning/ideas/backlog.md`
2. **Validated** → `.claude/enterprise/learning/best-practices/{topic}.md`
3. **Standard** → `.claude/enterprise/CLAUDE.md` (enforced by Claude)

See `.claude/enterprise/learning/PIPELINE.md` for full promotion criteria.

### Modifying Configuration

**Enterprise config (shared across all computers):**
```powershell
# Edit the master config
notepad $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\CLAUDE.md

# Commit
git add .claude/enterprise/CLAUDE.md
git commit -m "Update enterprise config: [what changed]"
git push
```

**Personal config (user preferences, tech stack):**
```powershell
# Edit personal layer
notepad $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\users\emarquez-personal\CLAUDE.md

# Commit
git add .claude/users/emarquez-personal/
git commit -m "Update personal prefs: [what changed]"
git push
```

**Machine-specific config (local paths only):**
```powershell
# This is auto-generated by setup-personal.ps1
# To regenerate:
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\scripts\setup-personal.ps1" -Force
```

---

## Key Files and Directories

| Path | Purpose | Sync Method |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `.claude/enterprise/CLAUDE.md` | Core workflow, principles, patterns | Git |
| `.claude/enterprise/commands/` | Custom `/commands` | Git |
| `.claude/enterprise/skills/` | Reusable workflows | Git |
| `.claude/enterprise/learning/` | Knowledge capture pipeline | Git |
| `.claude/enterprise/agents/` | Subagent definitions | Git |
| `.claude/enterprise/scripts/` | Setup and verification tools | Git |
| `.claude/users/emarquez-personal/CLAUDE.md` | Personal preferences | Git |
| `.claude/scripts/setup-personal.ps1` | Bootstrap script (called by machine-specific setup) | Git |
| `.claude/project-memory/` | Session state and project memory | Git (NEW: syncs across machines) |

**Do NOT commit to this repo:**
- Workspace-specific files from other projects
- API keys, credentials, secrets
- Large binary files
- Temporary test files

---

## Troubleshooting

### Symlinks Broken

**Symptom:** Commands don't work, verification fails

**Fix:**
```powershell
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\scripts\setup-personal.ps1" -Force
```

### Git Conflicts on Pull

**Symptom:** Merge conflicts when pulling changes

**Fix:**
```powershell
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git status          # Review conflicts
git stash           # Stash local changes if needed
git pull            # Pull clean
git stash pop       # Re-apply local changes
# Resolve conflicts manually, then:
git add .
git commit -m "Merge: resolve conflicts"
git push
```

### Different File Counts Between Computers

**Symptom:** Home has 13 commands, work has 8

**Fix:**
```powershell
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git fetch --all
git status  # Check if behind origin
git pull
```

### Commands Not Available After Pull

**Symptom:** Files exist but commands don't work in Claude Code

**Fix:** Restart Claude Code session or use `/clear` to reload

---

## Architecture Decisions

### Why Symlinks Instead of Git Clones?

**Problem:** Multiple repos (ClaudeHome, ClaudeWork, ClaudeDocs) would drift out of sync.

**Solution:** One shared repo (ClaudeDocs) symlinked into each machine's `~/.claude/`.
- Single source of truth
- Instant sync (no re-clone needed)
- Works with Obsidian vaults simultaneously

### Why Three Layers (Git + Symlinks + OneDrive)?

**Git:** Source of truth, cross-machine sync, version control
**Symlinks:** Runtime wiring (Claude Code reads from `~/.claude/`)
**OneDrive:** Optional visual editing in Obsidian, automatic backup

Each layer has a specific purpose and doesn't duplicate the others.

### Why Separate Enterprise and Personal Layers?

**Enterprise:** Shared workflow practices (Boris Cherny methodology, RPI pattern)
**Personal:** Tech stack, communication style, current focus areas
**Work:** Safari Circuits organizational standards (work computer only)

Separation allows:
- Reuse enterprise layer for other users/teams
- Customize personal prefs without affecting workflow
- Keep work context isolated to work machine

### Project Memory Sync (New)

Previously, `~/.claude/projects/` (project memory) was local-only.
Now it's symlinked to `.claude/project-memory/` and synced via Git.

**Benefit:** Session state and learnings follow you across machines.
**Trade-off:** Must manually resolve conflicts if working on same project from both computers simultaneously.

---

## Work Computer Integration (Safari Circuits)

On work computer (`emarquez` user), setup-personal.ps1 adds:

```powershell
~/.claude/safari/              → organizational-docs/.claude/
~/.claude/reference/safari/    → organizational-docs/shared/
~/.claude/CLAUDE.md includes   → @safari/CLAUDE.md
```

This wires organizational standards (branding, APIs, security policies) into Claude sessions.

**Requirement:** `organizational-docs` repo must exist at `~/organizational-docs`.

---

## References

- **Setup script:** `.claude/scripts/setup-personal.ps1`
- **Verification:** `.claude/enterprise/scripts/verify-sync.ps1`
- **Testing guide:** `.claude/enterprise/scripts/SYNC-TEST-GUIDE.md`
- **Learning pipeline:** `.claude/enterprise/learning/PIPELINE.md`
- **Enterprise config:** `.claude/enterprise/CLAUDE.md`
- **Personal config:** `.claude/users/emarquez-personal/CLAUDE.md`

**External sources:**
- [Boris Cherny's Claude Code workflow](https://x.com/bcherny/status/2007179832300581177)
- [Advanced Context Engineering](https://github.com/humanlayer/advanced-context-engineering-for-coding-agents)
- [12-Factor Agents](https://github.com/humanlayer/12-factor-agents)

input.content:
# Safari Circuits Project Workflow

How to create and manage Safari Circuits company projects using the personal vault + team SharePoint approach.

---

## Overview

**Privacy Model:**
- Work privately in personal Obsidian vault (`00_Claude_Safari\05-Projects`)
- Only publish finalized deliverables to team SharePoint
- Project memory syncs via Git (personal-claude-docs), not SharePoint

**Benefits:**
- Team doesn't see exploratory questions, rough drafts, or thinking process
- Can't accidentally share half-baked ideas
- Project memory follows you home ↔ work via Git
- Obsidian integration for linking, templates, graph view
- Clear publishing flow with explicit approval

---

## Creating a New Safari Project

### Step 1: Create Project in Personal Vault

```powershell
# Set variables
$projectName = "Safari-{Project-Name}"  # e.g., "Safari-CMMC-Level-2"
$vaultPath = "C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\00_Claude_Safari"
$projectPath = "$vaultPath\05-Projects\$projectName"

# Create project structure
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path "$projectPath\00-Inbox" -Force
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path "$projectPath\01-Research" -Force
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path "$projectPath\02-Drafts" -Force
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path "$projectPath\03-Deliverables" -Force

Write-Host "✓ Project created: $projectPath" -ForegroundColor Green
```

### Step 2: Create Project Files from Template

Use the template at: `personal-claude-docs/.claude/enterprise/reference/safari-project-template.md`

Copy and customize:

```powershell
# Copy template
$templatePath = "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\reference\safari-project-template.md"
$claudeMdPath = "$projectPath\CLAUDE.md"

Copy-Item $templatePath $claudeMdPath

# Edit with VS Code or Obsidian
code $claudeMdPath
# OR
obsidian://open?vault=00_Claude_Safari&file=05-Projects/$projectName/CLAUDE.md
```

Customize these sections:
- Project Overview (goal, timeline, stakeholders, status)
- Context for Claude (background, terminology, requirements)
- Team SharePoint Locations (where deliverables will be published)
- Common Tasks & Prompts (frequent operations)
- Decision Log (key decisions as project progresses)

### Step 3: Create SharePoint Links File

```powershell
# Create _links.md with SharePoint references
$linksTemplate = @"
# Team SharePoint Links & Publishing

## Published Project Folder (Team SharePoint)

**Primary Location:**
``````
S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Projects\{folder-name}
``````

> ⚠️ **IMPORTANT:** This is a TEAM SharePoint. Only publish finalized deliverables here.

## Publishing Workflow

[See Safari-CMMC-Level-2\_links.md for complete publishing instructions]

Quick publish command:
``````powershell
Copy-Item .\03-Deliverables\*.* 'S:\...\Projects\{folder-name}\' -Force
``````
"@

$linksTemplate | Out-File "$projectPath\_links.md" -Encoding utf8
```

### Step 4: Add PowerShell Alias

Edit your PowerShell profile (`$PROFILE`):

```powershell
function safari-{shortname} {
    cd "C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\00_Claude_Safari\05-Projects\Safari-{Project-Name}"
    claude
}
```

Example:
```powershell
function cmmc {
    cd "C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\00_Claude_Safari\05-Projects\Safari-CMMC-Level-2"
    claude
}
```

Then reload: `. $PROFILE`

Usage: Just type the alias to instantly open that project in Claude Code.

---

## Daily Workflow

### Working on the Project

```powershell
# Navigate to project (or use alias)
cd "C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\00_Claude_Safari\05-Projects\Safari-{Project-Name}"

# Start Claude Code
claude
```

**What happens:**
- Claude loads project-specific CLAUDE.md
- Memory accumulates at `~/.claude/projects/C--Users-...-{Project-Name}/`
- This memory syncs via personal-claude-docs Git (not SharePoint!)

### Capturing Ideas and Notes

```
User: "Note to self: Engineering team confirmed they need Gerber Viewer"
Claude: [Captures to 00-Inbox/]

User: "/learn We decided to prioritize MFA because it affects 15 CMMC controls"
Claude: [Captures to project memory]
```

### Creating Deliverables

```
User: "Create an executive presentation comparing the 3 vendors. Save to 02-Drafts/"
Claude: [Creates presentation-draft.pptx in 02-Drafts/]

User: "Update the presentation to emphasize software flexibility. Review and polish for board presentation."
Claude: [Iterates on draft]

User: "Looks good. Move to 03-Deliverables/ and prepare for publication."
Claude: [Moves file, updates filename to remove "draft"]
```

### Publishing to Team SharePoint

```powershell
# 1. Review deliverable one last time
Start-Process .\03-Deliverables\executive-summary.pptx

# 2. Create team folder (first time only)
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path 'S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Projects\{folder-name}' -Force

# 3. Copy to SharePoint
Copy-Item .\03-Deliverables\executive-summary.pptx `
          'S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Projects\{folder-name}\' -Force

# 4. Verify
explorer 'S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Projects\{folder-name}'

# 5. Email stakeholders
# Subject: {Project Name} - {Document Name} Available for Review
# Body: The {document name} is now available at S:\...\Projects\{folder-name}\{filename}
```

---

## Project Memory Sync

### How It Works

```
Personal Vault Project:
C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\00_Claude_Safari\05-Projects\Safari-CMMC-Level-2\
  ↓
Claude Code working directory
  ↓
Project memory stored at:
~/.claude/projects/C--Users-emarquez-OneDrive-...-00_Claude_Safari-05-Projects-Safari-CMMC-Level-2/
  ↓ symlink
personal-claude-docs/.claude/project-memory/C--Users-emarquez-OneDrive-...-00_Claude_Safari-05-Projects-Safari-CMMC-Level-2/
  ↓ Git sync
GitHub (personal-claude-docs repo)
  ↓ Git pull on other computer
Home computer gets same project memory!
```

### Syncing Across Computers

**On work computer (after working on project):**
```powershell
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git add .claude/project-memory/
git commit -m "Update: CMMC project progress"
git push
```

**On home computer:**
```powershell
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git pull

# Project memory is now synced!
cd "D:\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\01_Claude_Home\05-Projects\Safari-CMMC-Level-2"
claude
# Claude remembers all decisions and context from work computer
```

---

## Privacy Guidelines

### ✅ Safe to Publish (Team SharePoint)

- Final presentations
- Executive summaries
- Decision memos (approved)
- Cost comparisons (approved for sharing)
- Vendor comparison matrices
- Implementation timelines
- Board-ready documents

### ❌ Keep Private (Personal Vault Only)

- Rough drafts with comments
- Exploratory questions to Claude ("What if we chose CloudFit instead?")
- "What if" scenarios
- Budget sensitivity analysis
- Internal strategy discussions
- Vendor negotiation notes
- Personal opinions/concerns
- Work-in-progress documents
- Any document with "DRAFT" watermarks

### When in Doubt

**Ask:** "Would I be comfortable with the entire company seeing this?"
- **Yes:** Safe to publish to SharePoint
- **No:** Keep in personal vault

---

## Recommended Folder Structure

```
00_Claude_Safari/
└── 05-Projects/
    ├── Safari-CMMC-Level-2/
    │   ├── CLAUDE.md                     ← Project context + knowledge base
    │   ├── _links.md                     ← SharePoint locations + publish commands
    │   ├── 00-Inbox/                     ← Quick captures, meeting notes
    │   │   ├── README.md
    │   │   ├── vendor-questions-2026-05-15.md
    │   │   └── meeting-notes-2026-05-18.md
    │   ├── 01-Research/                  ← Source docs, analysis
    │   │   ├── README.md
    │   │   ├── vendor-proposals/
    │   │   ├── cost-analysis.xlsx
    │   │   └── requirements/
    │   ├── 02-Drafts/                    ← WIP documents (PRIVATE)
    │   │   ├── README.md
    │   │   ├── executive-summary-draft.pptx
    │   │   └── board-presentation-v2.pptx
    │   └── 03-Deliverables/              ← Ready to publish
    │       ├── README.md
    │       └── executive-summary.pptx
    ├── Safari-Azure-Migration/
    │   └── [same structure]
    └── Safari-BeFirst-Modernization/
        └── [same structure]
```

---

## Common Prompts

### Start New Project
```
I'm starting a new Safari project: [Project Name]. Help me create the directory structure
and customize the CLAUDE.md template with the following details: [context, goals, stakeholders].
```

### Generate Deliverable
```
Create a [presentation/report/memo] for [audience] covering [topic].
Include: [key points].
Save to 02-Drafts/[filename]-[date].
```

### Prepare for Publishing
```
Review the [document name] in 02-Drafts/. Remove any internal notes or rough thinking.
Polish for [audience] and move to 03-Deliverables/ when ready.
```

### Sync Project Memory
```
I'm switching to my home computer. Commit the project memory changes with message
"Update: [project name] progress - [what was accomplished]"
```

---

## Troubleshooting

### Project Memory Not Syncing

**Symptom:** Claude doesn't remember decisions from other computer

**Fix:**
```powershell
# 1. Verify symlink exists
Get-Item ~/.claude/projects
# Should show: Mode: l----- Target: personal-claude-docs/.claude/project-memory

# 2. Check git status
cd $env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs
git status
# Should show changes in .claude/project-memory/

# 3. Manually sync
git add .claude/project-memory/
git commit -m "Sync project memory"
git push
```

### Can't Access Team SharePoint from PowerShell

**Symptom:** `Copy-Item` fails with access denied

**Fix:**
1. Verify S: drive is mapped correctly
2. Check SharePoint permissions
3. Try opening in Explorer first: `explorer 'S:\...'`
4. Manually copy files if needed

### Vault Not Found

**Symptom:** `cd` command fails to find vault path

**Fix:**
```powershell
# Verify vault exists
Test-Path "C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\00_Claude_Safari"

# If not synced yet, wait for OneDrive sync
# Or check if path is different on this computer
```

---

## Quick Reference Card

| Task | Command |
|------|---------|
| **Start project** | `{alias}` (e.g., `cmmc`) |
| **Quick capture** | Add file to `00-Inbox/` |
| **Work on deliverable** | Create in `02-Drafts/` |
| **Publish to SharePoint** | Move to `03-Deliverables/`, then `Copy-Item` |
| **Sync memory** | `cd ~/personal-claude-docs && git add .claude/project-memory/ && git commit && git push` |
| **Open SharePoint** | `explorer 'S:\...\Projects\{folder}'` |

---

*This workflow keeps your thinking private while collaborating effectively with the team.*

## Privacy Guidelines

### ✅ Safe to Publish (Team SharePoint)

- Final presentations

content.text:
Perfect! The setup is working. Now let me confirm the complete workflow for Safari project ideas and how to use your PowerPoint template.

## 📋 Complete Workflow: New Safari Project Idea

### Phase 1: Project Creation (One-Time Setup)

```powershell
# 1. Set project details
$projectName = "Safari-CMMC-Level-2"  # Your project name
$vaultPath = "C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\00_Claude_Safari"
$projectPath = "$vaultPath\05-Projects\$projectName"

# 2. Create folder structure
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path "$projectPath\00-Inbox" -Force
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path "$projectPath\01-Research" -Force
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path "$projectPath\02-Drafts" -Force
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path "$projectPath\03-Deliverables" -Force

# 3. Create CLAUDE.md from template
Copy-Item "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\enterprise\reference\safari-project-template.md" `
          "$projectPath\CLAUDE.md"

# 4. Edit CLAUDE.md to add your project context
code "$projectPath\CLAUDE.md"
```

**In CLAUDE.md, you should:**
- Add project overview (goal, timeline, stakeholders)
- Add the vendor comparison data (paste the full CMMC comparison prompt you provided)
- Specify the PowerPoint template path
- Define common tasks (like "generate executive presentation")

### Phase 2: Working on the Project

```powershell
# Navigate to project
cd "$vaultPath\05-Projects\$projectName"

# Start Claude Code
claude
```

**Claude automatically loads:**
- Project-specific CLAUDE.md (with all your context)
- Project memory (remembers past decisions)
- Template references

### Phase 3: Requesting Deliverables (Presentations)

**Example prompt for a presentation:**

```
Create an executive presentation comparing the 3 CMMC vendors using the template at:
S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Grand Rapids IT Hub\10_Templates\01_PPT_Template.pptx

Focus on:
- The 15-point comparison matrix (make it readable, use color coding)
- True cost comparison (3-year TCO)
- Software flexibility (critical differentiator for Safari)
- Recommendation: Ardalyst for engineering needs

Format:
- Slide 1: Title
- Slide 2: Executive Summary (3-4 key points)
- Slide 3: Vendor Overview
- Slide 4-5: Comparison Matrix (split into two slides if needed)
- Slide 6: Cost Analysis (chart + table)
- Slide 7: Critical Issue - Software Flexibility
- Slide 8: Recommendation
- Slide 9: Next Steps

Save to: 02-Drafts/cmmc-vendor-comparison-2026-05-11.pptx
```

### Phase 4: Iteration & Refinement

```
Review the presentation in 02-Drafts/cmmc-vendor-comparison-2026-05-11.pptx.

Changes needed:
- Slide 4: Make the comparison table font bigger, bold the headers
- Slide 6: Add a bar chart showing 3-year costs side by side
- Slide 7: Add screenshots/mockups of Gerber Viewer to emphasize software needs

Update the file.
```

### Phase 5: Publishing

```powershell

# Safari Circuits - CMMC Level 2 Compliance Vendor Evaluation

## Project Overview

**Goal:** Evaluate and select CMMC Level 2 compliance vendor for Safari Circuits
**Timeline:** May 2026 - Q3 2026 (Decision: Week 4, Implementation: 30-90 days)
**Stakeholders:**
- Eduardo Marquez (IT Systems Engineering Manager) - Project Lead
- Scott (IT Director) - Approval Authority
- Engineering Team - Requirements Input
- External C3PAO Auditor - Assessment Partner

**Status:** Active - Vendor Evaluation Phase

---

## Context for Claude

Safari Circuits, LLC is a manufacturing/electronics company in Otsego, Michigan that:
- Manufactures circuit boards and handles CUI (Controlled Unclassified Information)
- Requires CMMC Level 2 certification for Department of Defense contracts
- Must comply with NIST 800-171 (110 security controls)
- Has 20 CUI users requiring secure access
- Needs specialized engineering software (Gerber Viewer for PCB design, potentially CAD tools)

### Critical Technical Requirements
- Microsoft 365 GCC-High environment
- Azure Virtual Desktop infrastructure
- Ability to install specialized engineering software
- Ongoing compliance management and C3PAO assessment support

---

## Workspace Structure

- **00-Inbox**: Quick captures, meeting notes, vendor emails, questions to process
- **01-Research**: Vendor proposals, analysis documents, reference materials
- **02-Drafts**: Work-in-progress presentations, comparison reports, decision memos
- **03-Deliverables**: Finalized outputs ready for team SharePoint (executive summaries, board presentations)

---

## Team SharePoint Locations

**Published deliverables:** `S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Projects\CMMC-Level-2`
**Policies:** `S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Policies\Security`
**Compliance:** `S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Compliance\Audits`

> **IMPORTANT:** Work happens privately in this vault. Only finalized deliverables get published to team SharePoint.

---

## Vendors Under Evaluation

### 1. ARDALYST (Tesseract Advanced Complete Plus)
- **Type:** Regional MSP (Otsego, Michigan)
- **Quote:** XH9OQ-4WTCD-BHGSC-NWG3G (dated 03-12-2026)
- **Contact:** Bethany Carter (bcarter@ardalyst.com, 540-476-2432)
- **3-Year Total:** $190,568
- **Key Feature:** Full-service package (M365 + AVD + support included)
- **Constraint:** 3-year mandatory commitment

### 2. SECUREFRAME (Complete + Defense Package)
- **Type:** National SaaS compliance platform (VC-backed)
- **Contact:** Andrew Mudiappu (andrew.mudiappu@secureframe.com, 415-578-1084)
- **3-Year Total:** $210,000 platform + ~$100K infrastructure = **~$310K+**
- **Key Feature:** Best-in-class automation, 200+ integrations
- **Constraint:** Software-only (must purchase M365/AVD separately)

### 3. CLOUDFIT SOFTWARE (easyCMMC)
- **Type:** Fully managed CMMC enclave (Lynchburg, Virginia)
- **Quote:** 20260511-194637581 (expires Jun 10, 2026)
- **Contact:** Jayson McFadden (jaysonmcfadden@cloudfitsoftware.com), Ian Hunt (ianhunt@cloudfitsoftware.com)
- **3-Year Total:** $180,000
- **Key Feature:** Lowest cost, fully managed, zero admin burden
- **Constraint:** NO admin access, "Standard Worker Persona" only (M365/Teams/Edge), custom software requires approval + extra cost

---

## Critical Decision Factors

### 1. SOFTWARE FLEXIBILITY (HIGHEST PRIORITY)

**The Question:** Can engineering team operate with ONLY M365/Teams/Edge, or do they need:
- ✅ Gerber Viewer (PCB design files)
- ✅ AutoCAD or CAD software
- ✅ ERP system integration
- ✅ Engineering calculators/simulation tools
- ✅ Version control clients (Git, SVN)
- ✅ Specialized manufacturing software

**Impact on Vendor Selection:**
- **If custom software needed:** ❌ CloudFit is NOT viable (approval delays, extra costs)
- **If only M365 needed:** ✅ CloudFit saves $10K-130K

### 2. ADMINISTRATIVE CONTROL vs. COST

| Vendor | Admin Control | 3-Year Cost | Trade-off |
|--------|--------------|-------------|-----------|
| **Ardalyst** | ⚠️ Moderate (admin access, MSP manages) | 💰 $191K | Balanced: reasonable control at reasonable cost |
