# Safari Circuits - CMMC Level 2 Compliance Vendor Evaluation

## Project Overview

**Goal:** Evaluate and select CMMC Level 2 compliance vendor for Safari Circuits
**Timeline:** May 2026 - Q3 2026 (Decision: Week 4, Implementation: 30-90 days)
**Stakeholders:**
- Eduardo Marquez (IT Systems Engineering Manager) - Project Lead
- Scott (IT Director) - Approval Authority
- Engineering Team - Requirements Input
- External C3PAO Auditor - Assessment Partner

**Status:** Active - Vendor Evaluation Phase

---

## Context for Claude

Safari Circuits, LLC is a manufacturing/electronics company in Otsego, Michigan that:
- Manufactures circuit boards and handles CUI (Controlled Unclassified Information)
- Requires CMMC Level 2 certification for Department of Defense contracts
- Must comply with NIST 800-171 (110 security controls)
- Has 20 CUI users requiring secure access
- Needs specialized engineering software (Gerber Viewer for PCB design, potentially CAD tools)

### Critical Technical Requirements
- Microsoft 365 GCC-High environment
- Azure Virtual Desktop infrastructure
- Ability to install specialized engineering software
- Ongoing compliance management and C3PAO assessment support

---

## Workspace Structure

- **00-Inbox**: Quick captures, meeting notes, vendor emails, questions to process
- **01-Research**: Vendor proposals, analysis documents, reference materials
- **02-Drafts**: Work-in-progress presentations, comparison reports, decision memos
- **03-Deliverables**: Finalized outputs ready for team SharePoint (executive summaries, board presentations)

---

## Team SharePoint Locations

**Published deliverables:** `S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Projects\CMMC-Level-2`
**Policies:** `S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Policies\Security`
**Compliance:** `S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Compliance\Audits`

> **IMPORTANT:** Work happens privately in this vault. Only finalized deliverables get published to team SharePoint.

---

## Vendors Under Evaluation

### 1. ARDALYST (Tesseract Advanced Complete Plus)
- **Type:** Regional MSP (Otsego, Michigan)
- **Quote:** XH9OQ-4WTCD-BHGSC-NWG3G (dated 03-12-2026)
- **Contact:** Bethany Carter (bcarter@ardalyst.com, 540-476-2432)
- **3-Year Total:** $190,568
- **Key Feature:** Full-service package (M365 + AVD + support included)
- **Constraint:** 3-year mandatory commitment

### 2. SECUREFRAME (Complete + Defense Package)
- **Type:** National SaaS compliance platform (VC-backed)
- **Contact:** Andrew Mudiappu (andrew.mudiappu@secureframe.com, 415-578-1084)
- **3-Year Total:** $210,000 platform + ~$100K infrastructure = **~$310K+**
- **Key Feature:** Best-in-class automation, 200+ integrations
- **Constraint:** Software-only (must purchase M365/AVD separately)

### 3. CLOUDFIT SOFTWARE (easyCMMC)
- **Type:** Fully managed CMMC enclave (Lynchburg, Virginia)
- **Quote:** 20260511-194637581 (expires Jun 10, 2026)
- **Contact:** Jayson McFadden (jaysonmcfadden@cloudfitsoftware.com), Ian Hunt (ianhunt@cloudfitsoftware.com)
- **3-Year Total:** $180,000
- **Key Feature:** Lowest cost, fully managed, zero admin burden
- **Constraint:** NO admin access, "Standard Worker Persona" only (M365/Teams/Edge), custom software requires approval + extra cost

---

## Critical Decision Factors

### 1. SOFTWARE FLEXIBILITY (HIGHEST PRIORITY)

**The Question:** Can engineering team operate with ONLY M365/Teams/Edge, or do they need:
- ✅ Gerber Viewer (PCB design files)
- ✅ AutoCAD or CAD software
- ✅ ERP system integration
- ✅ Engineering calculators/simulation tools
- ✅ Version control clients (Git, SVN)
- ✅ Specialized manufacturing software

**Impact on Vendor Selection:**
- **If custom software needed:** ❌ CloudFit is NOT viable (approval delays, extra costs)
- **If only M365 needed:** ✅ CloudFit saves $10K-130K

### 2. ADMINISTRATIVE CONTROL vs. COST

| Vendor | Admin Control | 3-Year Cost | Trade-off |
|--------|--------------|-------------|-----------|
| **Ardalyst** | ⚠️ Moderate (admin access, MSP manages) | 💰 $191K | Balanced: reasonable control at reasonable cost |
| **Secureframe** | ✅ Full (your infrastructure, your rules) | 💰💰💰 $310K+ | Maximum control, highest cost |
| **CloudFit** | ❌ None (CloudFit exclusive admin) | 💰 $180K | Cheapest, zero control |

### 3. NIST 800-171 COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS

If Safari has admin rights (Ardalyst/Secureframe), must implement:
- Change Management Process (CM.L2-3.4.3)
- Configuration Management (CM.L2-3.4.1, CM.L2-3.4.2)
- Least Functionality (CM.L2-3.4.6)
- Application Whitelisting (CM.L2-3.4.8)
- Software Integrity Verification (SI.L2-3.13.3)
- Vulnerability Management (RA.L2-3.11.2)
- Audit and Accountability (AU.L2-3.3.1)

**With CloudFit:** They manage all controls (Pro: no burden, Con: no visibility/control)

---

## 15-Point Vendor Comparison Matrix

| CRITERIA | ARDALYST | SECUREFRAME | CLOUDFIT |
|----------|----------|-------------|----------|
| **1. Year 1 Cost (20 users)** | $77,123 | $130,000+ | $60,000 |
| **2. 3-Year Total Cost** | $190,568 | $310,000+ | $180,000 |
| **3. M365 + AVD Included** | ✓ YES | ✗ NO (Buy separate) | ✓ YES |
| **4. Admin Control** | ✓ YOU HAVE ACCESS | ✓ FULL CONTROL | ✗ CLOUDFIT ONLY |
| **5. Install Custom Software** | ✓ YES (Your discretion) | ✓ YES (Your control) | ✗ APPROVAL REQUIRED (+Extra cost) |
| **6. IT Support / Help Desk** | ✓ INCLUDED | ✗ NOT INCLUDED | ✓ INCLUDED |
| **7. CMMC L2 Compliance** | ✓ FULL SUPPORT | ✓ FULL SUPPORT | ✓ FULL SUPPORT |
| **8. Compliance Automation** | ⚠️ BASIC (Manual processes) | ✓ ADVANCED (Comply AI, 200+ tests) | ⚠️ MANAGED SERVICE |
| **9. 24/7 Monitoring** | ⚠️ BUSINESS HOURS | ✓ 24/5 SUPPORT | ✓ 24/7 MONITORING |
| **10. Implementation Time** | 30-60 DAYS | 60-90 DAYS | 30 DAYS |
| **11. Contract Length** | ✗ 3 YEARS MANDATORY | ✓ 1 OR 3 YEARS | ✓ 1 YEAR AUTO-RENEW |
| **12. Software Flexibility** | ✓ FULL WINDOWS ENV | ✓ ANY SOFTWARE | ✗ M365/TEAMS/EDGE ONLY |
| **13. Scaling Users** | $1,614/user | $0 (up to 300 FTE) | $3,000/user |
| **14. Change Management** | ✓ YOUR PROCESS (MSP support) | ✓ YOUR PROCESS (Platform tracks) | ✗ CLOUDFIT APPROVAL |
| **15. Vendor Lock-In Risk** | ⚠️ MODERATE | ✓ LOW | ✗ HIGH |

---

## Common Tasks & Prompts

### Generate Executive Presentation
```
Create an executive summary presentation (5-7 slides) comparing the 3 CMMC vendors.
Include: problem statement, vendor overview, comparison matrix, cost analysis,
recommendation for Safari Circuits manufacturing needs, and next steps.
Save to 02-Drafts/executive-summary-[date].pptx
```

### Create Detailed Analysis Report
```
Generate a detailed vendor analysis report (15-20 slides) covering:
- Background (CMMC L2, Safari Circuits context)
- Each vendor's offering, pricing breakdown, pros/cons
- Side-by-side comparison matrix
- Software flexibility analysis (Gerber Viewer use case)
- NIST 800-171 compliance requirements
- Risk assessment and recommendation
Save to 02-Drafts/detailed-analysis-[date].pptx
```

### Draft Critical Questions for Vendors
```
Generate follow-up questions for [Vendor Name] based on the comparison analysis.
Focus on: software installation process, timeline, costs, customer references,
and specific concerns about [admin control / infrastructure / flexibility].
Save to 00-Inbox/questions-[vendor]-[date].md
```

### Create Cost Breakdown Spreadsheet
```
Build a detailed cost comparison spreadsheet showing:
- Year 1, Year 2, Year 3 costs for each vendor
- Hidden costs for Secureframe (M365, AVD, endpoint management, SIEM, IT support)
- Per-user scaling costs
- Total 3-year TCO comparison
Save to 01-Research/cost-analysis-[date].xlsx
```

### Generate Board Presentation
```
Create a board-level presentation (10-12 slides) for approval:
- Executive summary of CMMC L2 requirement
- Business impact (DoD contracts at risk)
- Vendor evaluation summary
- Financial comparison
- Recommended vendor with justification
- Implementation timeline and risks
Save to 02-Drafts/board-presentation-[date].pptx
```

### Draft Decision Memo
```
Write a decision memorandum for Scott (IT Director) recommending [Vendor Name].
Include: situation, analysis, alternatives considered, recommendation, and rationale.
Format for executive readability (1-2 pages max).
Save to 02-Drafts/decision-memo-[vendor]-[date].md
```

### Create Software Requirements Assessment

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Safari Circuits PowerPoint Generator
Generates PowerPoint presentations from YAML configuration files using Safari template.
"""

import json
import yaml
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from pptx import Presentation
from pptx.util import Inches, Pt
from pptx.enum.text import PP_ALIGN
from pptx.dml.color import RGBColor

# Safari Circuits brand colors (adjust based on actual template)
COLORS = {
    'green': RGBColor(76, 175, 80),    # Success/Advantage
    'yellow': RGBColor(255, 193, 7),   # Warning/Adequate
    'red': RGBColor(244, 67, 54),      # Error/Concern
    'blue': RGBColor(33, 150, 243),    # Info/Neutral
    'dark_gray': RGBColor(66, 66, 66), # Text
}

TEMPLATE_PATH = r"S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Grand Rapids IT Hub\10_Templates\01_PPT_Template.pptx"


def load_config(config_file: Path) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """Load presentation configuration from YAML or JSON file."""
    with open(config_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        if config_file.suffix in ['.yaml', '.yml']:
            return yaml.safe_load(f)
        elif config_file.suffix == '.json':
            return json.load(f)
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported config format: {config_file.suffix}")


def add_title_slide(prs: Presentation, config: Dict) -> None:
    """Add title slide."""
    slide = prs.slides.add_slide(prs.slide_layouts[0])  # Title slide layout

    title = slide.shapes.title
    subtitle = slide.placeholders[1]

    title.text = config.get('title', '')
    subtitle.text = config.get('subtitle', '')


def add_section_header(prs: Presentation, title: str) -> None:
    """Add section header slide."""
    slide = prs.slides.add_slide(prs.slide_layouts[2])  # Section header layout
    slide.shapes.title.text = title


def add_content_slide(prs: Presentation, slide_config: Dict) -> None:
    """Add content slide with title and bullet points."""
    slide = prs.slides.add_slide(prs.slide_layouts[1])  # Title and content layout

    title = slide.shapes.title
    title.text = slide_config.get('title', '')

    # Add content to text placeholder
    if 'content' in slide_config:
        body_shape = slide.placeholders[1]
        tf = body_shape.text_frame
        tf.clear()

        for item in slide_config['content']:
            p = tf.add_paragraph()
            p.text = item
            p.level = 0


def add_comparison_table(prs: Presentation, slide_config: Dict) -> None:
    """Add comparison table slide."""
    slide = prs.slides.add_slide(prs.slide_layouts[5])  # Blank layout

    # Add title
    title_shape = slide.shapes.title
    title_shape.text = slide_config.get('title', '')

    # Table dimensions
    rows = len(slide_config['rows']) + 1  # +1 for header
    cols = len(slide_config['columns'])

    # Create table
    left = Inches(0.5)
    top = Inches(1.5)
    width = Inches(9.0)
    height = Inches(0.5 * rows)

    table = slide.shapes.add_table(rows, cols, left, top, width, height).table

    # Set column headers
    for col_idx, col_name in enumerate(slide_config['columns']):
        cell = table.cell(0, col_idx)
        cell.text = col_name
        cell.text_frame.paragraphs[0].font.bold = True
        cell.text_frame.paragraphs[0].alignment = PP_ALIGN.CENTER

    # Fill table data
    for row_idx, row_data in enumerate(slide_config['rows'], start=1):
        for col_idx, cell_value in enumerate(row_data):
            cell = table.cell(row_idx, col_idx)

            # Handle colored cells (e.g., "🟢 YES", "🔴 NO")
            if isinstance(cell_value, str):
                cell.text = cell_value

                # Apply color coding
                if '🟢' in cell_value or 'YES' in cell_value.upper():
                    cell.fill.solid()
                    cell.fill.fore_color.rgb = COLORS['green']
                elif '🔴' in cell_value or 'NO' in cell_value.upper():
                    cell.fill.solid()
                    cell.fill.fore_color.rgb = COLORS['red']
                elif '🟡' in cell_value:
                    cell.fill.solid()
                    cell.fill.fore_color.rgb = COLORS['yellow']


def add_cost_chart_slide(prs: Presentation, slide_config: Dict) -> None:
    """Add cost comparison chart slide."""
    slide = prs.slides.add_slide(prs.slide_layouts[5])  # Blank layout

    title_shape = slide.shapes.title
    title_shape.text = slide_config.get('title', '')

    # For now, add as table - could be enhanced with actual charts
    # This would require more complex chart creation with python-pptx
    add_comparison_table(prs, slide_config)


def generate_presentation(config_file: Path, output_file: Path) -> None:
    """Generate PowerPoint presentation from configuration file."""

    print(f"Loading configuration from: {config_file}")
    config = load_config(config_file)

    print(f"Loading template from: {TEMPLATE_PATH}")
    prs = Presentation(TEMPLATE_PATH)

    # Add title slide
    if 'title_slide' in config:
        add_title_slide(prs, config['title_slide'])

    # Process slides
    for slide_config in config.get('slides', []):
        slide_type = slide_config.get('type', 'content')

        if slide_type == 'section_header':
            add_section_header(prs, slide_config['title'])
        elif slide_type == 'content':
            add_content_slide(prs, slide_config)
        elif slide_type == 'table':
            add_comparison_table(prs, slide_config)
        elif slide_type == 'cost_chart':
            add_cost_chart_slide(prs, slide_config)
        else:
            print(f"Warning: Unknown slide type '{slide_type}', skipping")

    # Save presentation
    print(f"Saving presentation to: {output_file}")
    prs.save(str(output_file))
    print("✓ Presentation generated successfully!")


def main():
    import argparse

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description='Generate Safari Circuits PowerPoint presentations from YAML/JSON config'
    )
    parser.add_argument('config', type=Path, help='Configuration file (YAML or JSON)')
    parser.add_argument('-o', '--output', type=Path, help='Output PPTX file')

    args = parser.parse_args()

    if not args.config.exists():
        print(f"Error: Configuration file not found: {args.config}")
        return 1

    if not args.output:
        args.output = args.config.with_suffix('.pptx')

    try:
        generate_presentation(args.config, args.output)
        return 0
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error generating presentation: {e}")
        import traceback
        traceback.print_exc()
        return 1


if __name__ == '__main__':
    exit(main())

toolUseResult.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Safari Circuits PowerPoint Generator
Generates PowerPoint presentations from YAML configuration files using Safari template.
"""

import json
import yaml
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from pptx import Presentation
from pptx.util import Inches, Pt
from pptx.enum.text import PP_ALIGN
from pptx.dml.color import RGBColor

# Safari Circuits brand colors (adjust based on actual template)
COLORS = {
    'green': RGBColor(76, 175, 80),    # Success/Advantage
    'yellow': RGBColor(255, 193, 7),   # Warning/Adequate
    'red': RGBColor(244, 67, 54),      # Error/Concern
    'blue': RGBColor(33, 150, 243),    # Info/Neutral
    'dark_gray': RGBColor(66, 66, 66), # Text
}

TEMPLATE_PATH = r"S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Grand Rapids IT Hub\10_Templates\01_PPT_Template.pptx"


def load_config(config_file: Path) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """Load presentation configuration from YAML or JSON file."""
    with open(config_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        if config_file.suffix in ['.yaml', '.yml']:
            return yaml.safe_load(f)
        elif config_file.suffix == '.json':
            return json.load(f)
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported config format: {config_file.suffix}")


def add_title_slide(prs: Presentation, config: Dict) -> None:
    """Add title slide."""
    slide = prs.slides.add_slide(prs.slide_layouts[0])  # Title slide layout

    title = slide.shapes.title
    subtitle = slide.placeholders[1]

    title.text = config.get('title', '')
    subtitle.text = config.get('subtitle', '')


def add_section_header(prs: Presentation, title: str) -> None:
    """Add section header slide."""
    slide = prs.slides.add_slide(prs.slide_layouts[2])  # Section header layout
    slide.shapes.title.text = title


def add_content_slide(prs: Presentation, slide_config: Dict) -> None:
    """Add content slide with title and bullet points."""
    slide = prs.slides.add_slide(prs.slide_layouts[1])  # Title and content layout

    title = slide.shapes.title
    title.text = slide_config.get('title', '')

    # Add content to text placeholder
    if 'content' in slide_config:
        body_shape = slide.placeholders[1]
        tf = body_shape.text_frame
        tf.clear()

        for item in slide_config['content']:
            p = tf.add_paragraph()
            p.text = item
            p.level = 0


def add_comparison_table(prs: Presentation, slide_config: Dict) -> None:
    """Add comparison table slide."""
    slide = prs.slides.add_slide(prs.slide_layouts[5])  # Blank layout

    # Add title
    title_shape = slide.shapes.title
    title_shape.text = slide_config.get('title', '')

    # Table dimensions
    rows = len(slide_config['rows']) + 1  # +1 for header
    cols = len(slide_config['columns'])

    # Create table
    left = Inches(0.5)
    top = Inches(1.5)
    width = Inches(9.0)
    height = Inches(0.5 * rows)

    table = slide.shapes.add_table(rows, cols, left, top, width, height).table

    # Set column headers
    for col_idx, col_name in enumerate(slide_config['columns']):
        cell = table.cell(0, col_idx)
        cell.text = col_name
        cell.text_frame.paragraphs[0].font.bold = True
        cell.text_frame.paragraphs[0].alignment = PP_ALIGN.CENTER

    # Fill table data
    for row_idx, row_data in enumerate(slide_config['rows'], start=1):
        for col_idx, cell_value in enumerate(row_data):
            cell = table.cell(row_idx, col_idx)

            # Handle colored cells (e.g., "🟢 YES", "🔴 NO")
            if isinstance(cell_value, str):
                cell.text = cell_value

                # Apply color coding
                if '🟢' in cell_value or 'YES' in cell_value.upper():
                    cell.fill.solid()
                    cell.fill.fore_color.rgb = COLORS['green']
                elif '🔴' in cell_value or 'NO' in cell_value.upper():
                    cell.fill.solid()
                    cell.fill.fore_color.rgb = COLORS['red']
                elif '🟡' in cell_value:
                    cell.fill.solid()
                    cell.fill.fore_color.rgb = COLORS['yellow']


def add_cost_chart_slide(prs: Presentation, slide_config: Dict) -> None:
    """Add cost comparison chart slide."""
    slide = prs.slides.add_slide(prs.slide_layouts[5])  # Blank layout

    title_shape = slide.shapes.title
    title_shape.text = slide_config.get('title', '')

    # For now, add as table - could be enhanced with actual charts
    # This would require more complex chart creation with python-pptx
    add_comparison_table(prs, slide_config)


def generate_presentation(config_file: Path, output_file: Path) -> None:
    """Generate PowerPoint presentation from configuration file."""

    print(f"Loading configuration from: {config_file}")
    config = load_config(config_file)

    print(f"Loading template from: {TEMPLATE_PATH}")
    prs = Presentation(TEMPLATE_PATH)

    # Add title slide
    if 'title_slide' in config:
        add_title_slide(prs, config['title_slide'])

    # Process slides
    for slide_config in config.get('slides', []):
        slide_type = slide_config.get('type', 'content')

        if slide_type == 'section_header':
            add_section_header(prs, slide_config['title'])
        elif slide_type == 'content':
            add_content_slide(prs, slide_config)
        elif slide_type == 'table':
            add_comparison_table(prs, slide_config)
        elif slide_type == 'cost_chart':
            add_cost_chart_slide(prs, slide_config)
        else:
            print(f"Warning: Unknown slide type '{slide_type}', skipping")

    # Save presentation
    print(f"Saving presentation to: {output_file}")
    prs.save(str(output_file))
    print("✓ Presentation generated successfully!")


def main():
    import argparse

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description='Generate Safari Circuits PowerPoint presentations from YAML/JSON config'
    )
    parser.add_argument('config', type=Path, help='Configuration file (YAML or JSON)')
    parser.add_argument('-o', '--output', type=Path, help='Output PPTX file')

    args = parser.parse_args()

    if not args.config.exists():
        print(f"Error: Configuration file not found: {args.config}")
        return 1

    if not args.output:
        args.output = args.config.with_suffix('.pptx')

    try:
        generate_presentation(args.config, args.output)
        return 0
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error generating presentation: {e}")
        import traceback
        traceback.print_exc()
        return 1


if __name__ == '__main__':
    exit(main())

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Safari Circuits PowerPoint Generator
Generates PowerPoint presentations from YAML configuration files using Safari template.
"""

import json
import yaml
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from pptx import Presentation
from pptx.util import Inches, Pt
from pptx.enum.text import PP_ALIGN
from pptx.dml.color import RGBColor

# Safari Circuits brand colors (adjust based on actual template)
COLORS = {
    'green': RGBColor(76, 175, 80),    # Success/Advantage
    'yellow': RGBColor(255, 193, 7),   # Warning/Adequate
    'red': RGBColor(244, 67, 54),      # Error/Concern
    'blue': RGBColor(33, 150, 243),    # Info/Neutral
    'dark_gray': RGBColor(66, 66, 66), # Text
}

TEMPLATE_PATH = r"S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Grand Rapids IT Hub\10_Templates\01_PPT_Template.pptx"


def load_config(config_file: Path) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """Load presentation configuration from YAML or JSON file."""
    with open(config_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        if config_file.suffix in ['.yaml', '.yml']:
            return yaml.safe_load(f)
        elif config_file.suffix == '.json':
            return json.load(f)
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported config format: {config_file.suffix}")


def add_title_slide(prs: Presentation, config: Dict) -> None:
    """Add title slide."""
    slide = prs.slides.add_slide(prs.slide_layouts[0])  # Title slide layout

    title = slide.shapes.title
    subtitle = slide.placeholders[1]

    title.text = config.get('title', '')
    subtitle.text = config.get('subtitle', '')


def add_section_header(prs: Presentation, title: str) -> None:
    """Add section header slide."""
    slide = prs.slides.add_slide(prs.slide_layouts[2])  # Section header layout
    slide.shapes.title.text = title


def add_content_slide(prs: Presentation, slide_config: Dict) -> None:
    """Add content slide with title and bullet points."""
    slide = prs.slides.add_slide(prs.slide_layouts[1])  # Title and content layout

    title = slide.shapes.title
    title.text = slide_config.get('title', '')

    # Add content to text placeholder
    if 'content' in slide_config:
        body_shape = slide.placeholders[1]
        tf = body_shape.text_frame
        tf.clear()

        for item in slide_config['content']:
            p = tf.add_paragraph()
            p.text = item
            p.level = 0


def add_comparison_table(prs: Presentation, slide_config: Dict) -> None:
    """Add comparison table slide."""
    slide = prs.slides.add_slide(prs.slide_layouts[5])  # Blank layout

    # Add title
    title_shape = slide.shapes.title
    title_shape.text = slide_config.get('title', '')

    # Table dimensions
    rows = len(slide_config['rows']) + 1  # +1 for header
    cols = len(slide_config['columns'])

    # Create table
    left = Inches(0.5)
    top = Inches(1.5)
    width = Inches(9.0)
    height = Inches(0.5 * rows)

    table = slide.shapes.add_table(rows, cols, left, top, width, height).table

    # Set column headers
    for col_idx, col_name in enumerate(slide_config['columns']):
        cell = table.cell(0, col_idx)
        cell.text = col_name
        cell.text_frame.paragraphs[0].font.bold = True
        cell.text_frame.paragraphs[0].alignment = PP_ALIGN.CENTER

    # Fill table data
    for row_idx, row_data in enumerate(slide_config['rows'], start=1):
        for col_idx, cell_value in enumerate(row_data):
            cell = table.cell(row_idx, col_idx)

            # Handle colored cells (e.g., "🟢 YES", "🔴 NO")
            if isinstance(cell_value, str):
                cell.text = cell_value

                # Apply color coding
                if '🟢' in cell_value or 'YES' in cell_value.upper():
                    cell.fill.solid()
                    cell.fill.fore_color.rgb = COLORS['green']
                elif '🔴' in cell_value or 'NO' in cell_value.upper():
                    cell.fill.solid()
                    cell.fill.fore_color.rgb = COLORS['red']
                elif '🟡' in cell_value:
                    cell.fill.solid()
                    cell.fill.fore_color.rgb = COLORS['yellow']


def add_cost_chart_slide(prs: Presentation, slide_config: Dict) -> None:
    """Add cost comparison chart slide."""
    slide = prs.slides.add_slide(prs.slide_layouts[5])  # Blank layout

    title_shape = slide.shapes.title
    title_shape.text = slide_config.get('title', '')

    # For now, add as table - could be enhanced with actual charts
    # This would require more complex chart creation with python-pptx
    add_comparison_table(prs, slide_config)


def generate_presentation(config_file: Path, output_file: Path) -> None:
    """Generate PowerPoint presentation from configuration file."""

    print(f"Loading configuration from: {config_file}")
    config = load_config(config_file)

    print(f"Loading template from: {TEMPLATE_PATH}")
    prs = Presentation(TEMPLATE_PATH)

    # Add title slide
    if 'title_slide' in config:
        add_title_slide(prs, config['title_slide'])

    # Process slides
    for slide_config in config.get('slides', []):
        slide_type = slide_config.get('type', 'content')

        if slide_type == 'section_header':
            add_section_header(prs, slide_config['title'])
        elif slide_type == 'content':
            add_content_slide(prs, slide_config)
        elif slide_type == 'table':
            add_comparison_table(prs, slide_config)
        elif slide_type == 'cost_chart':
            add_cost_chart_slide(prs, slide_config)
        else:
            print(f"Warning: Unknown slide type '{slide_type}', skipping")

    # Save presentation
    print(f"Saving presentation to: {output_file}")
    prs.save(str(output_file))
    print("✓ Presentation generated successfully!")


def main():
    import argparse

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description='Generate Safari Circuits PowerPoint presentations from YAML/JSON config'
    )
    parser.add_argument('config', type=Path, help='Configuration file (YAML or JSON)')
    parser.add_argument('-o', '--output', type=Path, help='Output PPTX file')

    args = parser.parse_args()

    if not args.config.exists():
        print(f"Error: Configuration file not found: {args.config}")
        return 1

    if not args.output:
        args.output = args.config.with_suffix('.pptx')

    try:
        generate_presentation(args.config, args.output)
        return 0
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error generating presentation: {e}")
        import traceback
        traceback.print_exc()
        return 1


if __name__ == '__main__':
    exit(main())

# General Docs Scripts

Reusable automation scripts for Eduardo's workflows.

**Location:** `C:\Users\emarquez\personal-claude-doc` (General Docs)

---

## PowerPoint Generator

**Script:** `generate_presentation.py`

Generates Safari Circuits PowerPoint presentations from YAML/JSON configuration files.

### Requirements

```powershell
py -m pip install python-pptx PyYAML
```

### Usage

```powershell
# Basic usage (output inferred from config filename)
py generate_presentation.py presentation-config.yaml

# Specify output file
py generate_presentation.py presentation-config.yaml -o output.pptx
```

### Configuration Format

YAML file structure:

```yaml
title_slide:
  title: "Presentation Title"
  subtitle: "Subtitle\nPresenter Name\nDate"

slides:
  - type: content
    title: "Slide Title"
    content:
      - "Bullet point 1"
      - "Bullet point 2"

  - type: table
    title: "Comparison Table"
    columns: ["Column 1", "Column 2", "Column 3"]
    rows:
      - ["Row 1 Col 1", "Row 1 Col 2", "Row 1 Col 3"]
      - ["Row 2 Col 1", "Row 2 Col 2", "Row 2 Col 3"]
```

### Supported Slide Types

| Type | Description | Required Fields |
|------|-------------|-----------------|
| `content` | Title + bullet points | `title`, `content` (list) |
| `table` | Comparison table | `title`, `columns`, `rows` |
| `section_header` | Section divider | `title` |

### Color Coding

The script automatically applies color coding to table cells:

| Pattern | Color | Use Case |
|---------|-------|----------|
| `🟢` or `YES` | Green | Advantage/Best |
| `🟡` | Yellow | Adequate/Acceptable |
| `🔴` or `NO` | Red | Concern/Issue |

### Template

Uses Safari Circuits official template:
```
S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Grand Rapids IT Hub\10_Templates\01_PPT_Template.pptx
```

### Example

CMMC Level 2 presentation example:
```powershell
cd "C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\00_Claude_Safari\05-Projects\Safari-CMMC-Level-2\02-Drafts"

py "C:\Users\emarquez\personal-claude-doc\scripts\generate_presentation.py" `
   cmmc-presentation-config.yaml `
   -o cmmc-executive-presentation-2026-05-11.pptx
```

---

## Future Scripts

Add additional reusable scripts here as they're created.

---

**Note:** This directory (`personal-claude-doc`) is separate from `personal-claude-docs` (plural), which is the Git-managed Claude Code configuration repository.

content.text:
# Learn - Capture Knowledge

Quickly capture a learning, correction, or insight into the appropriate location in your knowledge system.

## Purpose

**Accelerates knowledge capture** by:
- Providing a fast path to record learnings without disrupting flow
- Routing knowledge to the correct location (memory, CLAUDE.md, learning/, etc.)
- Ensuring insights don't get lost in conversation history
- Building a self-improving system over time

## When to Use

| Trigger | Action |
|---------|--------|
| "I learned that..." | Capture the learning |
| Correction given to Claude | Offer to capture |
| Pattern noticed 3+ times | Suggest formalizing |
| Mistake made | Learn from it |
| Successful approach validated | Document what worked |

## Usage

```
/learn [content]

Examples:
  /learn Use integration tests with real DB, not mocks
  /learn Odoo 18 uses <list> not <tree> in views
  /learn User prefers terse responses with no summaries
```

## Learning Types & Routing

| Type | When | Where | Format |
|------|------|-------|--------|
| **Feedback** | Correction or validated approach | memory/feedback_*.md | Rule + Why + How to apply |
| **Project** | Current work, goals, deadlines | memory/project_*.md | Fact + Why + How to apply |
| **User** | Preferences, role, expertise | memory/user_*.md | Profile information |
| **Reference** | External system pointers | memory/reference_*.md | Where to find things |
| **CLAUDE.md** | Codebase-specific patterns | project CLAUDE.md | Learnings section |
| **Ideas Backlog** | Future improvements | learning/ideas/backlog.md | Dated entry |
| **Best Practice** | Proven patterns (3+ uses) | learning/best-practices/ | Documented pattern |

## Workflow

1. **Capture** - Record the learning
2. **Route** - Determine correct location
3. **Format** - Structure appropriately
4. **Index** - Update MEMORY.md if needed
5. **Confirm** - Show what was captured

## Examples

### Feedback Memory
```markdown
User: "Don't create README files unless I ask"

/learn → Creates memory/feedback_no_auto_documentation.md:

---
name: No automatic documentation
description: Don't create README/guides unless explicitly requested
type: feedback
---

Never create documentation files (*.md, README) unless explicitly requested by the user.

**Why:** User wants documentation only when specifically asked for it, not proactively.

**How to apply:** Before creating any .md file, verify the user explicitly requested it. This applies to README.md, guides, tutorials, etc.
```

### Project Memory
```markdown
User: "We're freezing merges after Thursday for the mobile release"

/learn → Creates memory/project_merge_freeze.md:

---
name: Merge freeze for mobile release
description: Merge freeze begins 2026-05-12 for mobile release cut
type: project
---

Merge freeze begins 2026-05-12 (Thursday) for mobile team release branch cut.

**Why:** Mobile team is cutting a release branch and needs stability.

**How to apply:** Flag any non-critical PR work scheduled after 2026-05-12. Only critical fixes should be merged during freeze period.
```

### CLAUDE.md Learning
```markdown
User: "You should have read the existing code before making that change"

/learn → Adds to project CLAUDE.md Learnings section:

[2026-05-08] Always read existing files before modifying. Making changes without reading leads to breaking existing patterns and introducing bugs.
```

### Ideas Backlog
```markdown
User: "I wonder if we could auto-generate API docs from the code"

/learn → Adds to learning/ideas/backlog.md:

- [2026-05-08] Auto-generate API documentation from code annotations
  - Would reduce documentation drift
  - Need to research: JSDoc → Markdown tools
  - Complexity: Medium
  - Value: High (keeps docs in sync)
```

## Integration with Memory System

When creating memories:
1. Check if similar memory exists (update instead of duplicate)
2. Use semantic filenames (feedback_testing.md not feedback_1.md)
3. Keep MEMORY.md index under 200 lines
4. Verify memory after creation (show user what was saved)

## Natural Language Triggers

| User Says | Claude Does |
|-----------|-------------|
| "I learned that..." | Offer `/learn` or auto-capture |
| "Next time..." | Capture in ideas backlog |
| "Don't do X" | Create feedback memory |
| "Remember to..." | Create reference or project memory |
| Corrects Claude | Offer to capture correction |

## Related Commands

- `/reminder` - In-session action capture (different from learning)
- `/brain-dump` - Unstructured thought capture
- `/checkpoint` - Save session state
- `/memory` - View/edit memory system

## Quick Reference

```
Correction → memory/feedback_*.md
Project info → memory/project_*.md
Codebase pattern → CLAUDE.md learnings
Future idea → learning/ideas/backlog.md
Proven pattern (3+ uses) → learning/best-practices/
```

Generates PowerPoint presentations from YAML configuration files using Safari template.
"""

import json
import yaml
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Dict, List, Any

input.new_string:
def clean_template_slides(prs: Presentation) -> None:
    """Remove all slides from template except first and last."""
    slides_to_keep = []
    total_slides = len(prs.slides)

    if total_slides <= 2:
        # If 2 or fewer slides, keep all
        return

    # Keep first slide (index 0)
    slides_to_keep.append(0)

    # Keep last slide (index total_slides - 1)
    slides_to_keep.append(total_slides - 1)

    # Remove slides in reverse order (to maintain indices)
    for idx in range(total_slides - 1, -1, -1):
        if idx not in slides_to_keep:
            rId = prs.slides._sldIdLst[idx].rId
            prs.part.drop_rel(rId)
            del prs.slides._sldIdLst[idx]

    print(f"Cleaned template: kept {len(slides_to_keep)} slides (first and last)")


def generate_presentation(config_file: Path, output_file: Path) -> None:
    """Generate PowerPoint presentation from configuration file."""

    print(f"Loading configuration from: {config_file}")
    config = load_config(config_file)

    print(f"Loading template from: {TEMPLATE_PATH}")
    prs = Presentation(TEMPLATE_PATH)

    # Clean template slides (keep only first and last)
    clean_template_slides(prs)

    # Add title slide
    if 'title_slide' in config:
        add_title_slide(prs, config['title_slide'])

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Safari Circuits PowerPoint Generator
Generates PowerPoint presentations from YAML configuration files using Safari template.
"""

import json
import yaml
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Dict, List, Any
from pptx import Presentation
from pptx.util import Inches, Pt
from pptx.enum.text import PP_ALIGN
from pptx.dml.color import RGBColor

# Safari Circuits brand colors (adjust based on actual template)
COLORS = {
    'green': RGBColor(76, 175, 80),    # Success/Advantage
    'yellow': RGBColor(255, 193, 7),   # Warning/Adequate
    'red': RGBColor(244, 67, 54),      # Error/Concern
    'blue': RGBColor(33, 150, 243),    # Info/Neutral
    'dark_gray': RGBColor(66, 66, 66), # Text
}

TEMPLATE_PATH = r"S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Grand Rapids IT Hub\10_Templates\01_PPT_Template.pptx"


def load_config(config_file: Path) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """Load presentation configuration from YAML or JSON file."""
    with open(config_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        if config_file.suffix in ['.yaml', '.yml']:
            return yaml.safe_load(f)
        elif config_file.suffix == '.json':
            return json.load(f)
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported config format: {config_file.suffix}")


def add_title_slide(prs: Presentation, config: Dict) -> None:
    """Add title slide."""
    slide = prs.slides.add_slide(prs.slide_layouts[0])  # Title slide layout

    title = slide.shapes.title
    subtitle = slide.placeholders[1]

    title.text = config.get('title', '')
    subtitle.text = config.get('subtitle', '')


def add_section_header(prs: Presentation, title: str) -> None:
    """Add section header slide."""
    slide = prs.slides.add_slide(prs.slide_layouts[2])  # Section header layout
    slide.shapes.title.text = title


def add_content_slide(prs: Presentation, slide_config: Dict) -> None:
    """Add content slide with title and bullet points."""
    slide = prs.slides.add_slide(prs.slide_layouts[1])  # Title and content layout

    title = slide.shapes.title
    title.text = slide_config.get('title', '')

    # Add content to text placeholder
    if 'content' in slide_config:
        body_shape = slide.placeholders[1]
        tf = body_shape.text_frame
        tf.clear()

        for item in slide_config['content']:
            p = tf.add_paragraph()
            p.text = item
            p.level = 0


def add_comparison_table(prs: Presentation, slide_config: Dict) -> None:
    """Add comparison table slide."""
    slide = prs.slides.add_slide(prs.slide_layouts[5])  # Blank layout

    # Add title
    title_shape = slide.shapes.title
    title_shape.text = slide_config.get('title', '')

    # Table dimensions
    rows = len(slide_config['rows']) + 1  # +1 for header
    cols = len(slide_config['columns'])

    # Create table
    left = Inches(0.5)
    top = Inches(1.5)
    width = Inches(9.0)
    height = Inches(0.5 * rows)

    table = slide.shapes.add_table(rows, cols, left, top, width, height).table

    # Set column headers
    for col_idx, col_name in enumerate(slide_config['columns']):
        cell = table.cell(0, col_idx)
        cell.text = col_name
        cell.text_frame.paragraphs[0].font.bold = True
        cell.text_frame.paragraphs[0].alignment = PP_ALIGN.CENTER

    # Fill table data
    for row_idx, row_data in enumerate(slide_config['rows'], start=1):
        for col_idx, cell_value in enumerate(row_data):
            cell = table.cell(row_idx, col_idx)

            # Handle colored cells (e.g., "🟢 YES", "🔴 NO")
            if isinstance(cell_value, str):
                cell.text = cell_value

                # Apply color coding
                if '🟢' in cell_value or 'YES' in cell_value.upper():
                    cell.fill.solid()
                    cell.fill.fore_color.rgb = COLORS['green']
                elif '🔴' in cell_value or 'NO' in cell_value.upper():
                    cell.fill.solid()
                    cell.fill.fore_color.rgb = COLORS['red']
                elif '🟡' in cell_value:
                    cell.fill.solid()
                    cell.fill.fore_color.rgb = COLORS['yellow']


def add_cost_chart_slide(prs: Presentation, slide_config: Dict) -> None:
    """Add cost comparison chart slide."""
    slide = prs.slides.add_slide(prs.slide_layouts[5])  # Blank layout

    title_shape = slide.shapes.title
    title_shape.text = slide_config.get('title', '')

    # For now, add as table - could be enhanced with actual charts
    # This would require more complex chart creation with python-pptx
    add_comparison_table(prs, slide_config)


def generate_presentation(config_file: Path, output_file: Path) -> None:
    """Generate PowerPoint presentation from configuration file."""

    print(f"Loading configuration from: {config_file}")
    config = load_config(config_file)

    print(f"Loading template from: {TEMPLATE_PATH}")
    prs = Presentation(TEMPLATE_PATH)

    # Add title slide
    if 'title_slide' in config:
        add_title_slide(prs, config['title_slide'])

    # Process slides
    for slide_config in config.get('slides', []):
        slide_type = slide_config.get('type', 'content')

        if slide_type == 'section_header':
            add_section_header(prs, slide_config['title'])
        elif slide_type == 'content':
            add_content_slide(prs, slide_config)
        elif slide_type == 'table':
            add_comparison_table(prs, slide_config)
        elif slide_type == 'cost_chart':
            add_cost_chart_slide(prs, slide_config)
        else:
            print(f"Warning: Unknown slide type '{slide_type}', skipping")

    # Save presentation
    print(f"Saving presentation to: {output_file}")
    prs.save(str(output_file))
    print("[OK] Presentation generated successfully!")


def main():
    import argparse

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description='Generate Safari Circuits PowerPoint presentations from YAML/JSON config'
    )
    parser.add_argument('config', type=Path, help='Configuration file (YAML or JSON)')
    parser.add_argument('-o', '--output', type=Path, help='Output PPTX file')

    args = parser.parse_args()

    if not args.config.exists():
        print(f"Error: Configuration file not found: {args.config}")
        return 1

    if not args.output:
        args.output = args.config.with_suffix('.pptx')

    try:
        generate_presentation(args.config, args.output)
        return 0
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error generating presentation: {e}")
        import traceback
        traceback.print_exc()
        return 1


if __name__ == '__main__':
    exit(main())

# Scripts - General Docs

Reusable automation scripts for Eduardo's workflows.

**Location:** `C:\Users\emarquez\personal-claude-docs\scripts\`

---

## Safari PowerPoint Generator

**Script:** `generate_presentation.py`

Generates Safari Circuits PowerPoint presentations from YAML/JSON configuration files.

**Note:** This script is specifically for Safari Circuits work presentations using the company template.

### Requirements

```powershell
py -m pip install python-pptx PyYAML
```

### Usage

```powershell
# Basic usage (output inferred from config filename)
py generate_presentation.py presentation-config.yaml

# Specify output file
py generate_presentation.py presentation-config.yaml -o output.pptx
```

### Configuration Format

YAML file structure:

```yaml
title_slide:
  title: "Presentation Title"
  subtitle: "Subtitle\nPresenter Name\nDate"

slides:
  - type: content
    title: "Slide Title"
    content:
      - "Bullet point 1"
      - "Bullet point 2"

  - type: table
    title: "Comparison Table"
    columns: ["Column 1", "Column 2", "Column 3"]
    rows:
      - ["Row 1 Col 1", "Row 1 Col 2", "Row 1 Col 3"]
      - ["Row 2 Col 1", "Row 2 Col 2", "Row 2 Col 3"]
```

### Supported Slide Types

| Type | Description | Required Fields |
|------|-------------|-----------------|
| `content` | Title + bullet points | `title`, `content` (list) |
| `table` | Comparison table | `title`, `columns`, `rows` |
| `section_header` | Section divider | `title` |

### Color Coding

The script automatically applies color coding to table cells:

| Pattern | Color | Use Case |
|---------|-------|----------|
| `🟢` or `YES` | Green | Advantage/Best |
| `🟡` | Yellow | Adequate/Acceptable |
| `🔴` or `NO` | Red | Concern/Issue |

### Template

**Safari Circuits Official Template (Work presentations only):**
```
S:\OneDrive - Safari Circuits, LLC\Information Technology - Documents\Grand Rapids IT Hub\10_Templates\01_PPT_Template.pptx
```

The script automatically cleans the template, keeping only the first and last slides as defaults.

### Example

CMMC Level 2 presentation:
```powershell
cd "C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\00_Claude_Safari\05-Projects\Safari-CMMC-Level-2\02-Drafts"

py "C:\Users\emarquez\personal-claude-docs\scripts\generate_presentation.py" `
   cmmc-presentation-config.yaml `
   -o cmmc-executive-presentation-2026-05-11.pptx
```

---

## Future Scripts

Add additional reusable scripts here as they're created.

input.content:
# Safari Circuits Presentation Creation Workflow

**Status:** Validated - Successfully used for CMMC Level 2 vendor comparison (May 2026)

---

## Overview

Automated workflow for creating Safari Circuits executive presentations using configuration-driven approach.

## Successful Pattern

### 1. Research & Content Development
- Gather all data in project `01-Research/` folder
- Create detailed markdown outline in `02-Drafts/`
- Include all content: comparison tables, cost analysis, recommendations
- Format with clear structure (slide-by-slide breakdown)

### 2. Configuration File Creation
- Convert markdown outline to YAML configuration
- Structure: title_slide + slides array
- Slide types: `content`, `table`, `section_header`
- Use color coding: 🟢 (advantage), 🟡 (adequate), 🔴 (concern)

**Location:** Same folder as outline (`02-Drafts/`)

**Example:**
```yaml
title_slide:
  title: "Presentation Title"
  subtitle: "Presenter\nDate"

slides:
  - type: content
    title: "Slide Title"
    content:
      - "Bullet 1"
      - "Bullet 2"

  - type: table
    title: "Comparison"
    columns: ["Criteria", "Vendor A", "Vendor B"]
    rows:
      - ["Cost", "🟢 $100K", "🔴 $200K"]
```

### 3. Automated Generation
- Use script: `C:\Users\emarquez\personal-claude-docs\scripts\generate_presentation.py`
- Automatically cleans Safari template (keeps first/last slides)
- Generates color-coded tables
- Output to `02-Drafts/`

**Command:**
```powershell
cd "{project}\02-Drafts"
py "C:\Users\emarquez\personal-claude-docs\scripts\generate_presentation.py" `
   config.yaml -o presentation.pptx
```

### 4. Manual Review & Refinement
- User opens generated presentation
- Reviews content, formatting, flow
- Makes manual adjustments in PowerPoint
- Refines charts, adds graphics, adjusts layout

### 5. Finalization
- User moves final version to `03-Deliverables/`
- Renames with Safari naming convention: `YYYYMMDD Description.pptx`
- Ready for SharePoint publication

---

## Key Success Factors

| Factor | Why It Worked |
|--------|---------------|
| **Configuration-based** | Separates content from formatting; easy to iterate |
| **Template cleaning** | Starts fresh with only brand elements |
| **Color coding** | Visual consistency across comparison tables |
| **Two-phase approach** | Generate draft → Manual refinement |
| **Project structure** | Clear progression: Drafts → Deliverables |

---

## Learnings from CMMC Presentation

**What worked well:**
1. YAML config captured all content clearly
2. Comparison tables (15 criteria) rendered correctly with color coding
3. Template cleaning removed clutter
4. Manual refinement phase allowed for final polish
5. File size reduced from 1.5MB (uncleaned) to 136KB (final)

**Content structure that worked:**
- Executive Summary upfront (recommendation first)
- Context/background (why this matters)
- Vendor overview (high-level)
- Detailed comparisons (cost, features, criteria matrices)
- Clear recommendation with justification
- Why NOT alternatives (address objections)
- Next steps with timeline

**For future presentations:**
- Start with detailed markdown outline (easier to review/iterate)
- Create YAML config from outline
- Generate initial draft with script
- Allow time for manual refinement
- Use consistent naming: `YYYYMMDD Description.pptx`

---

## Related Files

- **Script:** `personal-claude-docs/scripts/generate_presentation.py`
- **Template:** `S:\...\Grand Rapids IT Hub\10_Templates\01_PPT_Template.pptx`
- **Example config:** `Safari-CMMC-Level-2\02-Drafts\cmmc-presentation-config.yaml`
- **Example outline:** `Safari-CMMC-Level-2\02-Drafts\executive-presentation-outline-2026-05-11.md`

---

## Future Enhancements

Ideas for improving the workflow:
- [ ] Support for charts (bar, pie) generation
- [ ] Image insertion from config
- [ ] Speaker notes generation
- [ ] Multiple template support (not just Safari)
- [ ] Reusable slide library (common slides)

---

**Last Updated:** 2026-05-12
**Validated By:** CMMC Level 2 Vendor Comparison presentation

# Learning to CLAUDE.md Enforcement Pipeline

## Overview

This pipeline ensures that best practices learned from videos, research, and experience are systematically promoted into CLAUDE.md files where they become **enforced team standards**.

```
┌─────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐
│   LEARN         │────▶│   DOCUMENT       │────▶│   PROMOTE       │────▶│   ENFORCE        │
│                 │     │                  │     │                 │     │                  │
│ - YouTube       │     │ - best-practices/│     │ - Review        │     │ - CLAUDE.md      │
│ - Articles      │     │ - youtube-       │     │ - Validate      │     │ - Skills         │
│ - Experience    │     │   analyses/      │     │ - Approve       │     │ - Hooks          │
└─────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘     └─────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘
```

## Stage 1: LEARN

**Input:** Raw content (videos, articles, trial-and-error)

**Actions:**
1. Watch/read content
2. Extract key insights
3. Note Safari-specific relevance

**Output:** Mental model of the practice

## Stage 2: DOCUMENT

**Input:** Key insights from learning

**Actions:**
1. Create file in `youtube-analyses/` or update `best-practices/{domain}.md`
2. Use standard templates with YAML metadata
3. Tag with `implementation_status: documented`
4. Rate `safari_relevance: high|medium|low`

**Output:** Documented practice with metadata

## Stage 3: PROMOTE

**Input:** Documented practice with `safari_relevance: high`

**Promotion Criteria:**
- [ ] Practice is validated (tested in real work)
- [ ] Practice has clear enforcement rules
- [ ] Practice applies across multiple projects
- [ ] Team agrees this should be standard

**Actions:**
1. Update `implementation_status: validated`
2. Write enforcement rule in promotion format:
   ```markdown
   ## [Practice Name]

   **Do:** [What to do]
   **Don't:** [What not to do]
   **Why:** [Reasoning]
   **Source:** [Link to learning doc]
   ```
3. Add to `~/.claude/learning/promoted/` queue

**Output:** Promotion-ready practice

## Stage 4: ENFORCE

**Input:** Promotion-ready practice

**Target Locations:**

| Scope | File | When to Use |
|-------|------|-------------|
| Global (all projects) | `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` | Universal practices |
| Safari projects | `safari-trace/.claude/CLAUDE.md` | Safari-specific |
| Specific domain | Project CLAUDE.md | Project-specific |

**Enforcement Levels:**

| Level | Format in CLAUDE.md | Claude Behavior |
|-------|---------------------|-----------------|
| **MUST** | "ALWAYS do X" / "NEVER do Y" | Claude will follow strictly |
| **SHOULD** | "Prefer X over Y" | Claude will default to this |
| **MAY** | "Consider X when..." | Claude will use judgment |

**Output:** Enforced practice in CLAUDE.md

## Promotion Template

When adding to CLAUDE.md, use this format:

```markdown
### [Practice Name]

**Rule:** [MUST|SHOULD|MAY] [action]

**Do:**
- Specific action 1
- Specific action 2

**Don't:**
- Anti-pattern 1
- Anti-pattern 2

**Why:** [Brief explanation]

**Source:** [Link to learning/best-practices/{file}.md]
```

## Tracking Promotions

Update `implementation-tracking/status.md` when promoting:

```markdown
| Practice | Source | Status | Promoted To | Date |
|----------|--------|--------|-------------|------|
| Component naming | ui-design.md | Enforced | global CLAUDE.md | 2026-01-18 |
```

## Quick Commands

### Promote a Practice
1. Verify practice in `best-practices/`
2. Write enforcement rule
3. Add to appropriate CLAUDE.md
4. Update tracking status

### Review Promoted Practices
```bash
# List all enforced rules across CLAUDE.md files
grep -r "MUST\|SHOULD\|NEVER\|ALWAYS" ~/.claude/*.md
grep -r "MUST\|SHOULD\|NEVER\|ALWAYS" ~/safari-trace/.claude/*.md
```

## Example Promotion

### Before (in best-practices/ui-design.md):
```markdown
## Component Naming
Use PascalCase for component names. Prefix Safari-specific components with "Safari".
Status: validated
```

### After (in CLAUDE.md):
```markdown
### Component Naming Convention

**Rule:** MUST use PascalCase for React components

**Do:**
- `SafariButton.tsx`
- `UserProfileCard.tsx`
- `DataTable.tsx`

**Don't:**
- `safari-button.tsx` (kebab-case)
- `safarButton.tsx` (camelCase)
- `Button.tsx` without Safari prefix for branded components

**Why:** Consistency across codebase, easy identification of Safari-branded vs generic components

**Source:** [best-practices/ui-design.md](~/.claude/learning/best-practices/ui-design.md)

content.text:
Excellent! I've reviewed the finalized presentation and captured comprehensive learnings for future presentation requests.

## What I Learned & Documented

**✓ Validated Presentation Workflow** (`learning/best-practices/safari-presentation-workflow.md`):

1. **Research & Content Development**
   - Gather data in `01-Research/`
   - Create detailed markdown outline in `02-Drafts/`
   - Structure with slide-by-slide breakdown

2. **Configuration-Based Generation**
   - Convert outline to YAML config
   - Use color coding (🟢🟡🔴) for visual consistency
   - Support content slides, comparison tables, section headers

3. **Automated Script Execution**
   - Template auto-cleans (keeps first/last slides only)
   - Generates draft with proper formatting
   - Output to `02-Drafts/` for review

4. **Manual Refinement Phase**
   - User opens, reviews, refines in PowerPoint
   - Adjusts layout, adds graphics, polishes content
   - Critical step for final quality

5. **Finalization**
   - Move to `03-Deliverables/`
   - Rename with Safari convention: `YYYYMMDD Description.pptx`
   - Ready for SharePoint publication

## Key Learnings from CMMC Presentation

**What worked exceptionally well:**
- YAML config separated content from formatting
- Comparison tables with 15 criteria rendered cleanly
- Template cleaning reduced file size (1.5MB → 136KB)
- Two-phase approach (automated + manual) balanced speed with quality
- Clear content structure: Summary → Context → Details → Recommendation → Next Steps

**Reusable Pattern Identified:**
- Executive Summary with recommendation upfront
- Why it matters (business context)
- Detailed comparisons with color-coded matrices
- Clear recommendation with justification
- Address alternatives/objections
- Timeline with next actions

## Committed to Git

All learnings are now synced and available across computers:
- ✓ `safari-presentation-workflow.md` (best practice)
- ✓ `generate_presentation.py` (automation script)
- ✓ `reference_general_docs.md` (location memory)
- ✓ Script documentation

**Future presentation requests:** I'll now follow this validated pattern automatically, adjusting content while maintaining the proven structure!
